app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.name: get_overlap_matrix
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Analyze a specific member's portfolio")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Analyze aggregated family portfolio")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.docstring:
Get the Mutual Fund Redundancy Matrix.

Identifies overlap between equity mutual funds in the user's (or family's) portfolio.
Returns pairs grouped by overlap severity (Extreme >75%, High 50-75%, Medium 25-50%, Low <25%).
Inlcudes stock-level breakdown for full transparency (Option B).

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.name: get_securities_overlap
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Analyze a specific member's portfolio")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Analyze aggregated family portfolio")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.docstring:
Get Securities Overlap Analysis.

Identifies overlapping securities across mutual funds, ETFs, and direct equity holdings.
Returns:
- Breakdown of Direct vs Indirect exposure
- Top 5 securities by total exposure
- Concentration risk alerts if any security > 10% of portfolio

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.name: get_family_overlap_analysis
params: family_id: int = Query(..., description="Family ID to analyze")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overla.docstring:
Get Detailed Family Overlap Analysis.

Aggregates securities overlap across all family members with detailed breakdown.
Returns:
- Total Family Exposure per security (% of Total Family Portfolio).
- Breakdown by Member (Direct vs Indirect).
- Details of funds contributing to indirect exposure.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.name: analysis_overview.py
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.docstring: Analysis overview endpoints serving analysis data for the analysis module.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.name: get_key_metrics
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch key metrics for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated key metrics for a family the user owns")
to_date: Optional[datetime] = Query(None, description="End date for key metrics calculation (defaults to current date, start date is always 1 year before)")
asset_class_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Asset class or Group ID to filter by")
entity_type: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Entity type: 'asset' or 'group'")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.docstring:
Get key metrics/analysis data for the authenticated user.
Calculates Alpha, Beta, Sharpe Ratio, and Max Drawdown using actual portfolio data.
Always calculates metrics for the 1-year period ending on the specified end date.

This endpoint is secured with:
1. JWT token authentication

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch key metrics for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch aggregated key metrics for (optional)
    to_date: End date for key metrics calculation (optional, defaults to current date)
             Start date is automatically set to 1 year before this date
    asset_class_id: Asset class ID or Group ID to filter by (optional)
    entity_type: Entity type - 'asset' for asset class filter, 'group' for group filter (optional)
    current_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: List of KeyMetricsResponse objects (Alpha, Beta, Sharpe Ratio, Max Drawdown)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.name: get_dividends
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch dividends for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated dividends for a family the user owns")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_overvi.docstring:
Get aggregated dividend history for the user/family (Last 1 Year).
Filtered by Payment Date (or Record Date fallback if unpaid).
Aggregated by Stock + Event.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_risk.p.docstring: Analysis risk endpoints serving risk metrics data for the analysis module.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_risk.p.name: get_risk_metrics
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch risk metrics for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated risk metrics for a family the user owns")
to_date: Optional[datetime] = Query(None, description="End date for risk metrics calculation (defaults to current date, start date is always 1 year before)")
asset_class_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Asset class or Group ID to filter by")
entity_type: Optional[str] = Query(None, description="Entity type: 'asset' or 'group'")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_risk.p.docstring:
Get risk metrics data for the authenticated user.
Calculates Volatility, Maximum Drawdown, Sharpe Ratio, Sortino Ratio, and Beta using actual portfolio data.
Always calculates metrics for the 1-year period ending on the specified end date.
Returns both portfolio and benchmark values for comparison.

This endpoint is secured with:
1. JWT token authentication

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch risk metrics for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch aggregated risk metrics for (optional)
    to_date: End date for risk metrics calculation (optional, defaults to current date)
             Start date is automatically set to 1 year before this date
    asset_class_id: Asset class ID or Group ID to filter by (optional)
    entity_type: Entity type - 'asset' for asset class filter, 'group' for group filter (optional)
    current_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: List of RiskMetric objects (Volatility, Max Drawdown, Sharpe Ratio, Sortino Ratio, Beta)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_risk.p.name: get_risk_metrics_chart
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/analysis_risk.p.docstring: Get risk metrics chart data comparing portfolio vs benchmark.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.docstring:
Create a new asset group for the authenticated user.

This endpoint creates a new asset group and requires:
1. JWT user authentication
2. Unique group name for the user

    current_user_id: Current authenticated user ID
    db: Database session

Returns:

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.name: get_asset_groups
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.docstring:
Get all asset groups created by the authenticated user.

This endpoint retrieves all asset groups for the authenticated user
with the count of assets in each group.
Asset group assets details are not included in the response.

Args:
    current_user_id: Current authenticated user ID
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: List of asset groups with asset_count (without asset_group_assets details)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.name: get_asset_group
params: asset_group_id: int = Path(..., description="ID of the asset group to retrieve")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.docstring:
Get a single asset group with its assets for the authenticated user.

This endpoint retrieves a single asset group including all associated asset_group_assets
and requires:
1. JWT user authentication
2. Ownership of the asset group

Args:
    asset_group_id: ID of the asset group to retrieve
    current_user_id: Current authenticated user ID
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: Asset group with its assets

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.name: update_asset_group
params: asset_group_id: int = Path(..., description="ID of the asset group to update")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.docstring:
Update an existing asset group for the authenticated user.

This endpoint updates an asset group and requires:
1. JWT user authentication
2. Ownership of the asset group
3. Valid user asset IDs if new assets are provided

Args:
    asset_group_id: ID of the asset group to update
    asset_group_data: Asset group update data
    current_user_id: Current authenticated user ID
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: Success message indicating asset group was updated

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.name: delete_asset_group
params: asset_group_id: int = Path(..., description="ID of the asset group to delete")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/asset_groups.py.docstring:
Permanently delete an asset group for the authenticated user.

This endpoint permanently deletes an asset group and requires:
1. JWT user authentication
2. Ownership of the asset group

All associated asset_group_assets will be automatically deleted (via CASCADE).

Args:
    asset_group_id: ID of the asset group to delete
    current_user_id: Current authenticated user ID
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: Success message indicating asset group was deleted

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/chat.py::send_c.name: send_chat_message
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/chat.py::send_c.docstring:
Send a chat message and receive a streaming SSE response.

NOTE: We do NOT use Depends(get_db) here because FastAPI disposes
dependency-injected sessions when the response *object* is returned,
which happens BEFORE streaming finishes. Instead, the streaming
wrapper below manages its own session lifecycle.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py.name: dashboard.py
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py.docstring:
Dashboard endpoints for portfolio and financial data.
These endpoints provide mock data for dashboard components.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.name: get_portfolio_summary
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch summary for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated summary for a family the user owns")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.docstring:
Get portfolio summary data for the authenticated user.

This endpoint returns lightweight summary items only for fast loading:
- Total Wealth
- YTD Return
- Cash Position

Heavy calculations are available via separate endpoints:
- /dashboard/volatility - For volatility calculation
- /dashboard/potential-savings - For potential savings and optimization opportunities

This endpoint is secured with:
1. JWT token authentication

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch summary for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch summary for (optional)
    current_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: Dictionary containing:
        - summary: List of PortfolioSummaryItem objects (total wealth, YTD return, cash position)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.name: get_volatility
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch volatility for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated volatility for a family the user owns")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.docstring:
Get portfolio volatility calculation.

This is a heavy calculation that processes 365 days of returns data.
Separated into its own endpoint for better performance and progressive loading.
Uses caching for fast subsequent requests.

This endpoint is secured with:
1. JWT token authentication

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch volatility for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch volatility for (optional)
    current_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: PortfolioSummaryItem with volatility data

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.name: get_potential_savings
params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch potential savings for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated potential savings for a family the user owns")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.docstring:
Get potential savings and optimization opportunities for the portfolio.

Potential savings is the sum of all optimization opportunity values.
This endpoint returns both the total potential savings and the breakdown
of optimization opportunities.

This is a heavy calculation that analyzes multiple optimization scenarios.
Separated into its own endpoint for better performance and progressive loading.

This endpoint is secured with:
1. JWT token authentication

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch potential savings for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch potential savings for (optional)
    current_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result: Dictionary containing:
        - potential_savings: PortfolioSummaryItem with total potential savings
        - optimization_opportunities: List of PortfolioSummaryItem objects with breakdown

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.name: get_current_value

params: member_user_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch tax breakdown for an accessible member")
family_id: Optional[int] = Query(None, description="Fetch aggregated tax breakdown for a family")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/dashboard.py::g.docstring:
Get tax breakdown for an individual member or aggregated for a family.

Returns net STCG, net LTCG, harvestable STCL, and harvestable LTCL.

Args:
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch tax breakdown for (optional)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.name: equity_price_history.py
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.docstring:
Equity Price History API Endpoints

Endpoints for syncing equity price history data from Morningstar API.
Focus: Data storage operations only (no query endpoints).

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.name: sync_historical_data
params: start_date: Optional[date] = Query(None, description="Optional: start date (defaults to 20 years ago)")
end_date: Optional[date] = Query(None, description="Optional: end date (defaults to today)")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.docstring:
Fetch historical price data from Morningstar API and sync to database.

- Syncs all symbols in securities_master table
- Historical data includes only OHLCV data (valuation metrics are NULL)
- Data range defaults to last 20 years
- Uses optimized parallel processing (50 API calls/sec, 10 DB connections)
decorators:
router.post(
    "/sync-historical",
    response_model=Result,
    summary="Sync historical price data from Morningstar API"
)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.name: sync_daily_data
params: batch_size: int = Query(50, ge=1, le=50, description="Number of symbols to process per API call (max: 50)")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.docstring:
Fetch today's price data from Morningstar API and sync to database.

- Syncs all symbols in securities_master table
- Includes OHLCV data + valuation metrics + trailing returns
- Processes 50 symbols per API call (optimized for Morningstar API)
decorators:
router.post(
    "/sync-daily",
    response_model=Result,
    summary="Sync daily price data from Morningstar API"
)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.name: sync_securities_from_api
params: exchange: str = Query("139", description="Exchange ID (default: 139 for BSE)")
security: str = Query("1", description="Security type (default: 1 for Equity)")
batch_size: int = Query(1000, ge=1, le=5000, description="Number of records to process per batch")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/equity_price_hi.docstring: Fetch securities from Morningstar API and sync to database.
decorators:
router.post(
    "/sync-securities",
    response_model=Result,
    summary="Sync securities from Morningstar API"
)

Get all accessible users and families that the authenticated user has access to view.

Returns a list where each item is either an individual user or a family.
Uses is_individual_user flag to distinguish between them.

This API considers all access scenarios:
1. Direct family membership (user is member of family)
2. Access through user_access table (user has explicit access to other users)
3. Family ownership (user owns families)

end_date: Optional[date] = Query(None, description="Last date to fill (default: today - 1 day)")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/pulse_lab.py::f.docstring:
Fill missing benchmark price history data from max_date + 1 to end_date (or today - 1 day).

This endpoint:
1. Finds the maximum date (max_date) for the benchmark
2. Generates missing data from max_date + 1 to end_date (or today - 1 day)
3. Fetches daily point-to-point returns from APIs (Pulse Lab primary, FinFactor fallback)
4. Calculates daily prices from returns
5. Stores the missing data

Args:
    benchmark_id: ID of the benchmark to fill gaps for
    end_date: Last date to fill (default: today - 1 day)
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Result object containing fill results and statistics

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py.name: reconcile.py
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py.docstring:
Equity, ETF, and MF Transaction Reconciliation API Endpoints

Endpoints for manually triggering equity, ETF, and MF transaction reconciliation for specific users.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::r.name: reconcile_equity_transactions
params: phone_number: str = Query(..., description="User's phone number")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::r.docstring:
Manually trigger equity transaction reconciliation for a specific user.

This endpoint:
1. Finds the user by phone number
2. Gets all demat accounts for that user
3. Reconciles transactions for each account (compares API vs DB)
4. Inserts missing transactions if found
5. Regenerates ledger if discrepancies are detected
decorators:
router.post(
    "/equity",
    response_model=Result,
    summary="Reconcile equity transactions for a user by phone number"
)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::_.name: _reconcile_account
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::_.docstring: Reconcile a single account and return result.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::r.name: reconcile_etf_transactions
params: phone_number: str = Query(..., description="User's phone number")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::r.docstring:
Manually trigger ETF transaction reconciliation for a specific user.

This endpoint:
1. Finds the user by phone number
2. Gets all ETF accounts for that user
3. Fetches linked accounts to get fi_data_id mapping
4. Reconciles transactions for each account (compares API vs DB)
5. Inserts missing transactions if found
6. Regenerates ledger if discrepancies are detected
decorators:
router.post(
    "/etf",
    response_model=Result,
    summary="Reconcile ETF transactions for a user by phone number"
)

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::_.name: _get_etf_fi_data_id_from_linked_accounts
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::_.docstring:
Get fi_data_id for an ETF account from linked accounts response.
Matches by demat_id or broker_code using pre-built maps for efficiency.

Args:
    etf_account: ETFAccounts object
    linked_accounts: Linked accounts response from API

Returns:
    fi_data_id string or None if not found

params: phone_number: str = Query(..., description="User's phone number")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/reconcile.py::r.docstring:
Manually trigger MF transaction reconciliation for a specific user.

This endpoint:
1. Finds the user by phone number
2. Reconciles transactions for the user (compares API vs DB)
3. Inserts missing transactions if found
4. Regenerates ledger if discrepancies are detected
decorators:
router.post(

    response_model=Result,
    summary="Reconcile MF transactions for a user by phone number"
)

Test endpoints for debugging.
These endpoints are secured with API key authentication.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::test_e.name: test_encrypt_decrypt
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::test_e.docstring:
[Test API] Encrypt or decrypt a string using the same algorithms as EncryptedString (GCM)
and DeterministicEncryptedString (SIV). Requires X-API-Key header.

- **action**: `encrypt` (plain text → ciphertext) or `decrypt` (ciphertext → plain text).
- **encryption_type**: `gcm` (EncryptedString, non-deterministic) or `siv` (DeterministicEncryptedString, deterministic).
- **text**: The text provided to encrypt (plain text) or decrypt (URL-safe base64 ciphertext).
decorators: router.post("/encryption", response_model=Result, summary="Encrypt or decrypt plain text (test only)")

params: exchangeid: str = Query("139", description="Exchange ID (default: 139 for BSE)")
type: str = Query("1", description="Type code (default: 1 for Equity)")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::initia.docstring:
Initial bulk fetch of all corporate actions for all stocks (historical data).

**Use this endpoint ONCE to populate the database with all historical corporate actions.**

Fetches all corporate actions (Dividend, Sub-Division, Bonus) from Morningstar API
for all securities in the database. Uses concurrent processing (50 req/sec).

Args:
    exchangeid: Exchange ID (default: 139 for BSE)
    type: Type code (default: 1 for Equity)

Returns:
    Result with bulk fetch statistics
decorators: router.post("/corporate-actions/initial-bulk-fetch", response_model=Result, summary="Initial bulk fetch of all corporate actions (historical data)")

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::daily_.name: daily_sync_corporate_actions
params: exchangeid: str = Query("139", description="Exchange ID (default: 139 for BSE)")
type: str = Query("1", description="Type code (default: 1 for Equity)")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::daily_.docstring:
Daily sync of corporate actions for all stocks.

Fetches only NEW corporate actions since yesterday. This is the main endpoint for daily maintenance.

**How it works:**
1. Fetches corporate actions from yesterday's date onwards
2. Only new actions are added to the database (existing ones are updated)
3. Uses concurrent processing (50 req/sec) for optimal performance

Args:
    exchangeid: Exchange ID (default: 139 for BSE)
    type: Type code (default: 1 for Equity)

Returns:
    Result with daily sync statistics
decorators: router.post("/corporate-actions/daily-sync", response_model=Result, summary="Daily sync of corporate actions (new data since yesterday)")

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::trigge.name: trigger_cost_basis_scheduler
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::trigge.docstring:
Manually trigger the Cost Basis Scheduler to update cost basis for all users.

This endpoint will:
- Update cost basis for all equity holdings (DematHoldings)
- Update cost basis for all MF accounts
- Update cost basis for all ETF holdings

The scheduler processes all users and their holdings to calculate and update
the cost basis values based on transaction history and corporate actions.
decorators: router.post("/trigger-cost-basis-scheduler", response_model=Result, summary="Trigger Cost Basis Scheduler manually")

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::trigge.name: trigger_daily_balance_scheduler

Useful for validating dividend generation immediately without waiting for the cron schedule.

app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::get_ho.name: get_holdings_match
params: user_id: int = Query(..., description="User ID to analyze")
filter_pledge_edis: bool = Query(False, description="Filter Pledge/EDIS noise (Balanced Pairs only)")
filter_ca_keywords: bool = Query(True, description="Filter CA keywords (BONUS, SPLIT, etc.) AND apply corporate action adjustments")
app/api/api_v1/endpoints/test.py::get_ho.docstring:
Compare holdings with transactions for a given user.

Replicates tax_optimisation logic:
1. Gets current holdings by security_id
2. Applies filters to transactions
3. If CA filter is enabled, also applies corporate action adjustments
4. Uses FIFO for SELL processing
5. Compares calculated units with actual holdings

Filter Options:
- filter_pledge_edis: Remove BY PLEDGE, BY EDIS transactions (duplicate BUY entries)
- filter_ca_keywords: Remove CA keyword transactions AND apply BONUS/SPLIT/CONSOLIDATION adjustments

Returns:
    Result with summary and individual holding comparisons
decorators: router.get("/holdings-match", response_model=Result, summary="Compare holdings with transactions")

app/cache/calculation_cache.py.name: calculation_cache.py
app/cache/calculation_cache.py.docstring:
Calculation cache service for expensive calculations (XIRR, Volatility, Tax Savings).

Redis-only caching strategy:
- Fast access for frequently requested data
- User-based key indexing for O(K) invalidation instead of O(N) SCAN operations

This provides:
- Fast reads (Redis cache)
- Fast invalidation (O(K) where K = keys per user, not all keys)
- Simple architecture (single source of truth)

app/cache/hybrid_cache.py.name: hybrid_cache.py
app/cache/hybrid_cache.py.docstring:
Hybrid cache implementation for Redis with user-based key indexing.

Provides O(K) invalidation instead of O(N) SCAN operations by tracking
cache keys per user in Redis sets.

app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache.name: HybridCache
app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache.docstring:
Async Redis hot cache with user-based key indexing.

- Canonical JSON format: {"value": ...}
- O(K) invalidation via Redis sets
- Uses synchronous Redis client (blocking operations in try/except)

app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.name: get
app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.docstring:
Get value from Redis cache.

Supports both float values (volatility, XIRR) and dict values (tax savings).

Args:
    key: Cache key

Returns:
    Cached value (float or dict) or None if not found

app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.name: set
app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.docstring:
Set value in Redis cache and track it in user key sets.

Supports both float values (volatility, XIRR) and dict values (tax savings).

Args:
    key: Cache key
    value: Value to cache (float or dict)
    ttl: Time to live in seconds
    user_ids: List of user IDs for key tracking
    namespace: Namespace for key tracking (default: 'keys')

app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.name: invalidate_users
app/cache/hybrid_cache.py::HybridCache::.docstring:
Invalidate all cache keys for given users.
Uses O(K) lookup via user key sets instead of O(N) SCAN.

Args:
    user_ids: List of user IDs to invalidate
    namespace: Namespace to invalidate (default: 'keys')

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis.name: Redis
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis.docstring:
Thin async wrapper around a synchronous redis-py client.

    Every I/O method runs the synchronous Redis call inside
    ``asyncio.to_thread()`` so it never blocks the event loop.
    This is critical: blocking the loop (especially with ``blpop``)
    prevents asyncpg from completing SSL handshakes when creating
    new database connections, causing permanent ConnectionDoesNotExistError.

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::exists.name: exists
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::exists.docstring:
Check if a key exists in Redis.

Args:
    key: Redis key

Returns:
    True if key exists, False otherwise

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::incr.name: incr
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::incr.docstring:
Increment a counter in Redis. If key doesn't exist, creates it with value 1.
Sets TTL on the key.

Args:
    key: Redis key
    ttl: Time to live in seconds (default: 86400 = 24 hours)

Returns:
    New value after increment

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::rpush.name: rpush
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::rpush.docstring:
Push a value to the right end of a Redis list (enqueue).

Args:
    key: Redis list key
    value: Value to push (will be converted to string)

Returns:
    New length of the list

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::blpop.name: blpop
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::blpop.docstring:
Blocking pop from the left end of Redis list(s) (dequeue).

Args:
    keys: List of Redis list keys to pop from
    timeout: Timeout in seconds (default: 5)

Returns:
    Tuple of (key, value) if item found, None if timeout

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::setnx.name: setnx
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::setnx.docstring:
Set key to value only if key does not exist (atomic lock acquisition).

Args:
    key: Redis key
    value: Value to set
    ttl: Time to live in seconds (default: 300 = 5 minutes)

Returns:
    True if key was set (lock acquired), False if key already exists (lock not acquired)

app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::delete.name: delete
app/cache/redis_cache.py::Redis::delete.docstring:
Delete a key from Redis (lock release).

Args:
    key: Redis key to delete

Returns:
    Number of keys deleted (0 or 1)

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py.name: crest_ai_crud.py

CRUD operations for CREST AI chatbot queries.

This module provides READ-ONLY database query methods optimized for
the AI chatbot tool calls. Formatting and business logic remain in
the service layer (crest_ai_services.py).

Return calculations use adjusted_close prices to account for splits/dividends:
  return = (latest_adjusted_close - prior_adjusted_close) / prior_adjusted_close * 100

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::_get_prior_ta.name: _get_prior_target_date

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::_build_prior_.name: _build_prior_prices_cte

Build a CTE that fetches the closest adjusted_close per security_id
on or before `prior_target` (with 30-day buffer for holidays/gaps).
Uses PostgreSQL DISTINCT ON for efficiency.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD.name: CrestAICRUD
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD.docstring: Read-only CRUD operations for CREST AI chatbot queries.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_latest_trade_date
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring: Get the latest trade date from equity_price_history.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_latest_term_deposits
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring: Get latest term deposit record per account_ref_number.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_latest_recurring_deposits
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring: Get latest recurring deposit record per account_ref_number.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_fund_holdings_by_isin
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring:
Get underlying holdings of a fund for the latest snapshot date.
Returns (holdings_list, as_on_date).

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_top_equity_performers
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring:
Get top/bottom performing equities. Returns (rows, trade_date).
Each row has: isin_code, symbol, close_price, volume, market_cap,
              return_value, security_name, sector_name.

Returns are computed from adjusted_close prices:
  return = (latest_adjusted - prior_adjusted) / prior_adjusted * 100
For '1d', uses pre-computed daily_change_percentage (already adjusted).

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_top_fund_performers
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring:
Get top/bottom performing MFs/ETFs. Returns (rows, cutoff_date).
Calculates returns from latest NAV vs prior-period NAV.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_sector_market_performance
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring:
Get market-cap-weighted sector returns. Returns (rows, trade_date).
Each row has: sector, weighted_return, stock_count.

Returns computed from adjusted_close prices for accuracy.

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_user_equity_with_market_cap
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring:
Get user's demat equity holdings joined with market cap from
equity_price_history on the given trade date. Uses DISTINCT ON
to get latest holdings per (user, demat_account, isin).

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_user_mf_with_master
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring: Get user's MF holdings joined with mf_etf_master (isin, sub_category, scheme_name, value).

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_user_etf_with_master
app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.docstring: Get user's ETF holdings joined with mf_etf_master (isin, sub_category, scheme_name, value).

app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py::CrestAICRUD::.name: get_benchmark_trailing_returns

Query underlying holdings for given fund ISINs and compute
market-cap-weighted distribution per fund.

Non-equity holdings (cash, bonds, international) are skipped.

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: upsert_cash_flows_batch
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Execute upsert for a single batch of cash flows (low-level database operation).

Args:
    db: Database session
    batch: List of record dicts to upsert (already formatted with timestamps)
    current_time: Current timestamp for created_at/updated_at

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: get_cash_flows_for_xirr
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Get cash flows for XIRR calculation.

Date boundary rules:
- Start date: Valuation is included, but cash flows on start_date are EXCLUDED
- Cash flows: Include only from (start_date + 1 day) to (as_of_date - 1 day)
- End date: Valuation is included, but cash flows on as_of_date are EXCLUDED

This prevents double-counting: both start_date and end_date valuations already reflect 
all activity up to those dates, so cash flows on those dates should not be included.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs (for family/group calculations)
    as_of_date: Calculate XIRR up to this date (valuation included, cash flows excluded)
    asset_class_id: Optional filter by asset class ID
    demat_id: Optional filter by demat_id (only for EQUITY)
    mf_etf_master_id: Optional filter by mf_etf_master_id (for MF/ETF)
    security_id: Optional filter by security_id (for EQUITY per-holding XIRR)
                When provided, aggregates across all demat accounts for that security
    start_date: Optional start date filter (for time-period XIRR)
               If provided, returns cash flows from (start_date + 1 day) to (as_of_date - 1 day)

Returns:
    List of dicts with keys: flow_date, cash_flow_amount (aggregated across all users)

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: get_portfolio_cash_flows
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Get aggregated cash flows across all asset classes for portfolio XIRR.

Date boundary rules:
- Start date: Cash flows on start_date are EXCLUDED (to prevent double-counting)
- Cash flows: Include only from (start_date + 1 day) to (as_of_date - 1 day)
- End date: Cash flows on as_of_date are EXCLUDED (to prevent double-counting)

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs (for family/group calculations)
    as_of_date: Calculate XIRR up to this date (valuation included, cash flows excluded)
    start_date: Optional start date filter (for time-period XIRR)
               If provided, returns cash flows from (start_date + 1 day) to (as_of_date - 1 day)

Returns:
    List of dicts with keys: flow_date, cash_flow_amount (aggregated across all users)

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: get_equity_cash_flows_by_demat
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Get cash flows for a specific demat account (equity only).

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs (for family/group calculations)
    demat_id: Demat account ID
    as_of_date: Calculate XIRR up to this date
    equity_asset_class_id: Asset class ID for equity

Returns:
    List of dicts with keys: flow_date, cash_flow_amount (aggregated across all users)

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: aggregate_equity_transactions_chunk
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Aggregates equity transactions for a date chunk using market prices
from equity_price_history with nearest-prior-date lookup.

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: aggregate_mf_transactions_chunk
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Aggregate MF transactions by user, mf_etf_master_id, and date for a date chunk.
When mf_account_id is provided, only that FI account's transactions are aggregated.

Returns data already formatted for bulk upsert (includes user_id, asset_class_id, mf_etf_master_id, mf_account_id when provided).

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: aggregate_etf_transactions_chunk
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Aggregate ETF transactions by user, mf_etf_master_id, and date for a date chunk.
When etf_account_id is provided, only that etf_account's transactions are aggregated.

Joins with mf_etf_master table to get mf_etf_master.id from ISIN.
Groups by mf_etf_master_id to provide individual ETF-level granularity.

Returns data already formatted for bulk upsert (includes user_id, asset_class_id, mf_etf_master_id, etf_account_id when provided).

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: delete_cash_flows_for_account_date_range
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Delete all cash flow entries for a specific account within a date range.
Uses hard delete (not soft delete) since we're regenerating cash flows.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Demat account ID
    asset_class_id: Asset class ID (e.g., EQUITY)
    start_date: Start date (inclusive)
    end_date: End date (inclusive)

Returns:
    Number of rows deleted

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: delete_cash_flows_for_etf_user_date_range
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Delete all ETF cash flow entries for a user within a date range.
When etf_account_id is provided, delete only rows for that ETF account (DailyCashFlows.etf_account_id).
Uses hard delete (not soft delete) since we're regenerating cash flows.

app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.name: delete_cash_flows_for_mf_user_date_range
app/crud/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCashF.docstring:
Delete all MF cash flow entries for a user within a date range.
When mf_account_id is provided, delete only rows for that FI account (DailyCashFlows.mf_account_id).
Uses hard delete (not soft delete) since we're regenerating cash flows.

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py.name: dashboard_crud.py
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py.docstring:
CRUD operations for dashboard aggregations.

IMPORTANT: When adding a new asset type:
1. Add the asset type to AssetTypes in app/constants/dashboard_constants.py
2. Add a new CTE (Common Table Expression) in the SQL queries below
3. Add the new column to the SELECT statement
4. Add the new key to the return dictionary

Return keys use BASE keys only (no suffix):
- deposits (AssetTypes.DEPOSITS.key)
- recurring (AssetTypes.RECURRING.key)
- term (AssetTypes.TERM.key)
- nps (AssetTypes.NPS.key)
- equity (AssetTypes.EQUITY.key)

The service layer adds suffixes (_now, _one_year, _april) as needed.

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: DashboardCRUD
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
CRUD operations for dashboard calculations.

Note: Returns base keys (e.g., 'deposits', 'equity') without suffixes.
The service layer transforms these using AssetTypes helpers.

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_current_balances
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get current balances for all asset types with optional filters.
Supports filtering by specific asset IDs for Groups.

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_cash_mf_balance
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get current balance for liquid mutual funds  (Fund name like 'Liquid' or 'Overnight' or 'Arbitrage') and ETFS have fund name like 'Liquid' or 'liquid'

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs

Returns:
    Total balance of liquid mutual funds and etfs

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_balances_as_of
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get balances for deposits, recurring deposits, term deposits, NPS, and equity with optional
start/end filters. When no dates are provided, falls back to current balances.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    start_date: Optional lower bound for transactions (inclusive). 
               If date object or datetime with time 00:00:00, will be converted to end of day (23:59:59.999999)
    end_date: Optional upper bound for transactions (inclusive).
             If date object or datetime with time 00:00:00, will be converted to end of day (23:59:59.999999)
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (e.g., "equity", "deposits")
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs to filter by (e.g., demat_id, mf_id)
Returns:
    Dictionary with base keys (no suffix):
        - deposits
        - recurring
        - term
        - nps
        - equity
        - etf
        - mf

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_earliest_balances
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get earliest available balances by finding the minimum transaction date across ALL asset types,
then fetching balances for ALL assets on that single minimum date.

This provides a true "inception" point - the first day any investment was made,
and the portfolio value on that day across all asset types.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (e.g., "equity", "deposits")
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs to filter by (e.g., demat_id for equity/mf)
Returns:
    Dictionary with base keys (no suffix):
        - deposits
        - recurring
        - term
        - nps
        - equity
        - etf
        - mf

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_earliest_holdings_date
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get the earliest holdings date for each asset class (Equity, MF, ETF).

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    asset_class_type: Optional filter by asset class type (e.g., 'equity', 'mf', 'etf')
                      If None, returns dates for all asset classes
    demat_id: Optional filter by demat_id (only for EQUITY)

Returns:
    Dictionary with keys: 'equity', 'mf', 'etf' and their earliest dates (or None if not found)
    If asset_class_type is specified, only that key will have a value, others will be None

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_top_holdings
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get top holdings from deposits, term deposits, recurring deposits,
NPS, equities, and ETFs. Returns top N holdings per asset type.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    limit: Number of top holdings to return per asset type (default: 5)
    requester_user_id: ID of the user request to determine "You" label

Returns:
    List of dictionaries with holding information including ownerName

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_returns_for_volatility
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Fetch portfolio returns data for volatility calculation.

Calculates value-weighted portfolio returns for each day based on market movements only.
Handles sparse ledgers (transaction-based) by reconstructing daily positions using
generate_series and joining with price/NAV history tables.

Process:
1. Reconstructs daily holdings for Equity, MF, and ETF from ledger entries
2. Calculates individual asset returns from price/NAV changes (market movements only)
3. Calculates portfolio weights (asset value / total portfolio value)
4. Computes value-weighted portfolio return: Σ(weight_i × return_i)

For non-trading days (weekends/holidays), uses 0% return (no market movement).
Missing NAVs for MF are handled by excluding that asset from the day's calculation.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    start_date: Start date for returns calculation
    end_date: Optional end date for returns calculation
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (equity, mf, etf)
    equity_account_ids: Optional list of demat account IDs to filter equity by
    mf_account_ids: Optional list of MF account IDs to filter MF by
    etf_account_ids: Optional list of ETF account IDs to filter ETF by

Returns:
    List of RowMapping objects with columns: 'date', 'portfolio_return'
    - date: Calendar date
    - portfolio_return: Value-weighted portfolio return in percentage (float or None)

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_asset_allocation_breakdown
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get detailed asset allocation breakdown by account/security/fund.
Returns None if no asset_class_type (use get_balances_as_of instead).

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs
    end_date: Optional end date (defaults to current date). 
            If date object or datetime with time 00:00:00, will be converted to end of day (23:59:59.999999)
    asset_class_type: Asset class type to get breakdown for
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs to filter by (Legacy)
    ... specific ID lists for filtering

Returns:
    List of dicts with keys: 'name', 'value' (balance), or None if no asset_class_type

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_daily_balances_with_manual
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get daily balances for multiple users on a specific date, aggregated by asset_class_type.
Combines data from user_daily_balances (automated) and user_assets (manual).

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_min_max_transaction_dates
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Get the oldest and latest transaction dates across all financial tables for a list of users.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of user IDs to query

Returns:
    Tuple of (oldest_date, latest_date) across all users and all tables

Tables checked:
- bank_statements (transaction_timestamp)
- demat_account_transactions (transaction_datetime)
- etf_user_account_statements (transaction_datetime)
- mf_user_account_statements (transaction_datetime)
- recurring_deposits_transactions (transaction_datetime)
- term_deposits_transactions (transaction_datetime)

app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.name: get_holdings_count_by_type
app/crud/dashboard_crud.py::DashboardCRU.docstring:
Count distinct holdings per asset type (Equity, MF, ETF).
Each count includes manual assets of that same type.

Counts:
- equity: Distinct stocks per user per demat account (units > 0) + manual equity assets
- mf: Distinct MF schemes per user (total_units > 0) + manual MF assets
- etf: Distinct ETFs per user per account (total_units > 0) + manual ETF assets

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_ids: List of target user IDs

Returns:
    Dict with keys 'equity', 'mf', 'etf' each mapping to their holding count

app/crud/demat_holdings.py::DematHolding.name: delete_holdings_not_in_isins
app/crud/demat_holdings.py::DematHolding.docstring:
Delete demat holding records where ISIN is NOT in the current ISIN list.
This is a single-query approach that efficiently removes sold stocks.

SQL Equivalent:
DELETE FROM demat_holdings
WHERE demat_account_id = ?
  AND isin NOT IN (current_isins)

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    current_isins: List of ISINs from current API response (holdings still held)

Returns:
    Number of holdings deleted (sold stocks)

app/crud/demat_holdings.py::DematHolding.name: get_sector_distribution_for_users
app/crud/demat_holdings.py::DematHolding.docstring:
Get sector distribution for equity holdings for a list of users.
Joins securities_master via security_id (consistent with rest of codebase).
Holdings with no mapped sector fall under 'Others'.
Returns a list of dicts with user_id, sector, and value.

Note: Uses raw text() query to avoid SQLAlchemy creating two separate
bind parameters for the 'Others' literal inside COALESCE, which causes
PostgreSQL to reject the GROUP BY expression as different from the SELECT.

Get total valuation for a specific equity security and user on a given date.
Aggregates across all demat accounts for that security.

For today's date: Uses current holdings table (demat_holdings) with units * last_traded_price
For historical dates: Uses ledger with units * price from equity_price_history

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    security_id: Security ID from securities_master
    valuation_date: Date to get valuation for

Returns:
    Total valuation as float (0.0 if no holdings found)

Resync equity_price_history_id_seq if it is behind max(id).
External processes (pg_restore, COPY, migrations) may insert with explicit id,
leaving the sequence behind and causing duplicate key errors.

app/crud/equity_price_history.py::Equity.name: bulk_upsert
app/crud/equity_price_history.py::Equity.docstring:
Bulk upsert price history records with batching to avoid PostgreSQL parameter limits.

- ON CONFLICT (security_id, trade_date) DO UPDATE
- Sets created_at if missing, always refreshes updated_at
- Batches to stay safely under the 65,535 parameter limit

exists in multiple demat accounts.

for historical values. Otherwise uses current live etf_holdings.

This ensures user_daily_balances.account_identifier = etf_accounts.id.

app/crud/etf_holdings_fund.py::ETFHoldin.docstring: Delete all ETF holdings fund records for an ISIN

app/crud/etf_holdings_fund.py::ETFHoldin.docstring: Get the latest updated_at timestamp for a given ETF ISIN

    end_date: End date (inclusive)

    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Optional account ID to filter by

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.name: fetch_price_history_by_security_ids

Fetch price data for security_ids within date range (trading days only).

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::g.docstring:
Get the most recent ledger date for a specific user (optionally filtered by account).

    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Optional account ID to filter by

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::g.name: get_first_ledger_date_by_user
app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::g.docstring:
Get the oldest ledger date for a specific user (optionally filtered by account).

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Optional account ID to filter by

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.name: fetch_ledger_entries_at_date

    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    ledger_date: Ledger date to fetch
    account_id: Optional account ID to filter by (if processing single account)

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.name: fetch_latest_ledger_entries_per_isin
app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.docstring:
Fetch the most recent ledger entries for each ISIN on or before target_date.
Ensures that securities not updated recently are not dropped in forward generation.
Returns tuples: (user_id, id, etf_account_id, isin, scheme_name, etf_holdings_id, units, ledger_date)

app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.name: fetch_all_etfs_for_ledger
app/crud/etf_ledger.py::ETFLedgerCRUD::f.docstring:
Fetch all ETFs for ledger calculation - includes both:
1. Current holdings from etf_holdings (with etf_holdings_id and current_units)
2. Previously sold ETFs from transactions (etf_holdings_id = None, current_units = 0)

Only includes ETFs with valid security_id (present in securities_master).
Uses security_id as the primary identifier (stable across ISIN changes).

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID

Get total valuation for a specific ETF and user on a given date.
Aggregates across all ETF accounts for that ETF.

For today's date: Uses current holdings table (etf_holdings) with current_value
For historical dates: Uses ledger with units * NAV from historic_nav

    user_id: User ID
    isin: ISIN of the ETF
    valuation_date: Date to get valuation for

Returns:

Upsert one row for (user_id, fi_type, account_id).
Only columns passed as non-None are written; on conflict, only those columns are updated.
Commits so callers can rely on persistence without an explicit commit.

app/crud/fi_account_sync_status.py::FiAc.docstring:
Upsert only processing date/status columns for (user_id, fi_type, account_id).
Used by account_processing_status_worker. Only columns passed as non-None are updated.
Commits so worker does not need to commit.

app/crud/fi_account_sync_status.py::FiAc.name: upsert_checklist_batch_for_consent
app/crud/fi_account_sync_status.py::FiAc.docstring:
Bulk upsert checklist rows for consent ACTIVE/REJECTED (Step 3a).
Each item in accounts must have fiType, accountId; optionally maskedAccountNumber, fipId.
One round-trip and one commit for the whole batch.

app/crud/fi_account_sync_status.py::FiAc.docstring:
Set account_display_name only if the row exists and account_display_name is currently null or empty.
Returns True if an update was performed, False if skipped (already set or row missing).
Commits only when an update is performed.

The response_payload is encrypted at rest using AES-256-GCM with FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY.

app/crud/mf_ledger.py::MFLedgerCRUD::fet.docstring:

1. Current holdings from mf_holdings (with mf_holdings_id and current_units)
2. Previously sold funds from transactions (mf_holdings_id = None, current_units = 0)
When account_id is provided, scope holdings and transaction-derived funds to that account's mf_account_ids.
Returns combined list of (mf_holdings_id, user_id, mf_account_id, isin, scheme_code, fund_name, current_units, folio)

app/crud/mf_ledger.py::MFLedgerCRUD::get.name: get_valuation_by_isin_and_user
app/crud/mf_ledger.py::MFLedgerCRUD::get.docstring:
Get total valuation for a specific MF fund and user on a given date.
Aggregates across all MF accounts for that fund.

For today's date: Uses current holdings table (mf_accounts) with current_value
For historical dates: Uses ledger with units * NAV from historic_nav

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    isin: ISIN of the mutual fund
    valuation_date: Date to get valuation for

Returns:
    Total valuation as float (0.0 if no holdings found)

app/crud/security_lookup.py.name: security_lookup.py
app/crud/security_lookup.py.docstring:
Security Lookup CRUD

Provides functions to resolve ISIN codes to stable security_id (from securities_master).
Handles ISIN changes from corporate actions (splits, mergers, etc.).

Usage:
    security_id = await security_lookup_crud.get_security_id_by_isin(db, "INE009A01021")

app/crud/security_lookup.py::SecurityLoo.name: SecurityLookupCRUD
app/crud/security_lookup.py::SecurityLoo.docstring:
CRUD operations for resolving ISIN to security_id.

ISIN codes can change due to corporate actions (splits, mergers, re-listing).
This class provides a centralized way to find the stable security_id.

Lookup order:
1. securities_master.isin_code (current ISIN)
2. corporate_actions.new_isin (ISIN after corporate action)
3. corporate_actions.old_isin (ISIN before corporate action)

Returns:
    Dict mapping ISIN -> security_id (or None if not found)

app/crud/user_dividends.py::UserDividend.name: get_aggregated_dividends
app/crud/user_dividends.py::UserDividend.docstring:
Fetch aggregated dividends for a list of users (Family View).
Filters logic:
1. User must be in user_ids.
2. Date Logic (Last 1 Year):
   - Condition A: Payment Date exists AND is between (Today - 365) and Today.
   - Condition B: Payment Date is NULL AND Record Date is between (Today - 380) and (Today - 15).
     (Logic: If payment date missing, we use record_date with 15-day lag assumption)

Aggregation:
- Group by Security + Record Date.
- Sum Units Held and Total Amount.
- Order by Payment Date DESC (Nulls Last), then Record Date.

Execute *func()* with exponential-backoff retries on connection errors.

        Early retries rely on pool_pre_ping to replace bad connections.
        On the second-to-last attempt the pool is disposed so the final
        retry gets a completely fresh connection.

        ``func`` must be a **callable** returning a new coroutine each time
        (e.g. a lambda), NOT a pre-created coroutine.

Establish one connection at startup so the pool is not cold.

        allowing the worker to start (subsequent jobs will surface the issue).

app/db/encrypted_types.py.name: encrypted_types.py
app/db/encrypted_types.py.docstring:
SQLAlchemy custom TypeDecorators for transparent field-level encryption.

Usage in models:
    from app.db.encrypted_types import EncryptedString, DeterministicEncryptedString, EncryptedDateTime

    class MyModel(Base):
        # For PII fields never used in WHERE clauses (AES-256-GCM, non-deterministic):
        full_name = Column(EncryptedString(255), nullable=True)

        # For fields used in WHERE / equality lookups (AES-256-SIV, deterministic):
        phone_number = Column(DeterministicEncryptedString(15), nullable=False, unique=True, index=True)

        # For datetime PII fields:
        holder_dob = Column(EncryptedDateTime(), nullable=True)

The underlying DB column type remains String / Text, so NO schema migration is needed.
Values are stored as URL-safe base64-encoded ciphertext.

app/db/encrypted_types.py::_get_key.name: _get_key
app/db/encrypted_types.py::_get_key.docstring: Lazy-import settings and return the raw encryption key bytes.

app/db/encrypted_types.py::EncryptedStri.name: EncryptedString
app/db/encrypted_types.py::EncryptedStri.docstring:
AES-256-GCM encrypted string column.

Use for PII fields that are NEVER compared in WHERE clauses (e.g., full_name,
holder_name, masked_acc_number). Each write generates a fresh nonce, so the
same plaintext produces a different ciphertext each time.

DB column type: String (base64 ciphertext is longer than the original — size
parameter is advisory; underlying column stores the encoded blob).

app/db/encrypted_types.py::Deterministic.name: DeterministicEncryptedString
app/db/encrypted_types.py::Deterministic.docstring:
AES-256-SIV deterministic encrypted string column.

Use for fields that appear in WHERE clauses, indexes, or GROUP BY (e.g.,
phone_number, pan_number, isin, demat_id, account_id). The same plaintext
always encrypts to the same ciphertext with the same key, so:

    WHERE phone_number = encrypt_siv('9876543210')

continues to work correctly without any CRUD changes.

DB column type: Text (base64 SIV ciphertext).

app/db/encrypted_types.py::EncryptedDate.name: EncryptedDateTime
app/db/encrypted_types.py::EncryptedDate.docstring:
AES-256-GCM encrypted datetime column.

Stores the datetime as an ISO-8601 string, encrypts it, and decrypts /
parses it back to a timezone-aware datetime on read.

Use for PII datetime fields (e.g., holder_dob) that are never compared
directly in SQL.

DB column type: Text.

app/db/encrypted_types.py::EncryptedDate.name: process_bind_param

Daily cash flows table - unifies cash flows from all asset classes (Equity, MF, ETF).

This table aggregates transactions by day, making XIRR calculations fast and efficient.
For equity transactions, demat_id is required. For MF and ETF, demat_id is NULL.

Cash flow sign convention:
- BUY / SIP → negative (money out)
- SELL / REDEEM / DIV → positive (money in)

Equity Price History Model

Stores historical and daily price data for equities.
Data is fetched from Morningstar API:
- Historical data: Using IndexTS endpoint (OHLCV only)
- Daily updates: Using Index.php endpoint (OHLCV + valuation metrics + returns)

The table is designed to handle:
1. Bulk historical data backfill (20 years default)
2. Incremental daily updates with additional metrics

Fields:
- OHLCV: Always present from both APIs
- Valuation metrics: Only from daily API (NULL for historical data)
- Trailing returns: Only from daily API (NULL for historical data)

app/scheduler/benchmark_price_history_sc.name: benchmark_price_history_scheduler.py
app/scheduler/benchmark_price_history_sc.docstring:
Benchmark Price History Daily Scheduler

Scheduled task to update benchmark price history data daily.
Runs daily to fetch and store the previous day's benchmark returns.

This scheduler:
1. Runs daily at a specified time (default: 8:00 AM IST)
2. Fetches daily returns for the previous day only (not today)
3. Updates price history for all benchmarks in BENCHMARK_CODE_MAP
4. Handles errors gracefully and continues with other benchmarks

app/scheduler/benchmark_price_history_sc.name: start_scheduler_daily
app/scheduler/benchmark_price_history_sc.docstring:
Start the scheduler to run daily at the specified time.

APScheduler calculates 'missed' based on timing:
1. When add_job() is called → calculates next_run_time (e.g., 21:18:00.000)
2. When scheduler.start() is called → internal loop begins checking jobs
3. Internal loop checks every few seconds → compares current_time vs next_run_time
4. If current_time > next_run_time → logs "missed" (but still runs if within grace period)

The "missed" calculation happens from when APScheduler's internal loop checks,
NOT from server start time. The delay is between add_job() and when the loop checks.

Solution: Start scheduler FIRST, then add jobs to minimize delay window.

Args:
    hour: Hour in IST (24-hour format, default: 8)
    minute: Minute (default: 0)

app/scheduler/cost_basis_scheduler.py.name: cost_basis_scheduler.py
app/scheduler/cost_basis_scheduler.py.docstring:
Cost Basis Scheduler

Scheduled task to recalculate and store cost basis for all active users' holdings.
Runs daily at configured time (e.g. 6:00 AM IST).

This scheduler follows the same pattern as daily_balance_scheduler:
1. Uses a single database session for the entire job
2. Fetches active users in batches via users_crud.get_active_user_ids
3. For each batch: calculates cost basis (equity, MF, ETF) and persists in place
4. Commits after each batch; logs batch progress and final totals
5. Guards against overlapping runs with _is_running

Uses single session, active-user batching, and batch commit (same pattern as daily balance).

Updates ORM objects in place; caller commits the session.
Returns count of holdings/accounts updated.

app/scheduler/daily_balance_scheduler.py.docstring:
Daily Balance Snapshot Scheduler

Scheduled task to capture daily balance snapshots for all users.
Runs daily at 11:00 PM IST to store current balances in user_daily_balances table.

This scheduler:
1. Fetches all active users from the database
2. Retrieves granular current balances for each user across all asset types (separate entry per account)
3. Maps asset types to their generic asset_class_id
4. Stores snapshots in user_daily_balances table
5. Runs daily at 11 PM IST

Process all failed events with SCHEDULED status.
Runs every hour. Uses a short-lived DB session per event so the connection
is returned to the pool before/after each callback (which may do API calls).
Uses centralized run_with_db_retry for transient connection error recovery.

app/scheduler/failed_events_scheduler.py.name: start_scheduler
app/scheduler/failed_events_scheduler.py.docstring:
Start the scheduler to run every hour.

APScheduler calculates 'missed' based on:
1. When add_job() is called, it calculates next_run_time (e.g., 23:00:00)
2. When scheduler.start() is called, APScheduler's internal loop begins
3. The internal loop checks jobs periodically (every few seconds)
4. If current_time > next_run_time when checked, it logs "missed"

With misfire_grace_time configured, jobs still run even if "missed" by a few seconds.
The "missed" message is just a warning - the job will still execute within grace period.

app/scheduler/transaction_reconciliation.name: transaction_reconciliation_scheduler.py
app/scheduler/transaction_reconciliation.docstring:
Transaction Reconciliation Scheduler

Weekly scheduler that reconciles equity, ETF, and MF transactions by comparing API transactions
with DB transactions for all users with demat, ETF, or MF accounts.

Process:
1. Process equity users separately (in batches)
2. Process ETF users separately (in batches)
3. Process MF users separately (in batches)
4. For each account/user, process yearwise (2025 → 2005):
   - Fetch API transactions for year
   - Fetch DB transactions for year
   - Compare and find missing transactions
   - Insert missing transactions
   - Update cash flows
5. If discrepancies found:
   - Delete cash flows from max discrepancy month end to inception
   - Delete ledger from max discrepancy month end to inception
   - Regenerate ledger (backward generation)

Runs weekly on Sunday at 3:00 AM IST by default.

app/scheduler/transaction_reconciliation.name: TransactionReconciliationScheduler

Main reconciliation job that runs weekly.

Processes equity, ETF, and MF separately in batches, comparing API
transactions with DB transactions yearwise and regenerating ledger
when discrepancies are found.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: account_processing_status_service.py
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Account processing status service – business logic for the account event checklist API
and for webhook-driven checklist updates (consent, FI events, handler failures).

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _parse_date
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Parse ISO date string to date object. Returns None if invalid or None.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: apply_account_processing_status_update
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Apply account processing status update directly to DB (no queue).
Replaces enqueue_account_processing_status_update when worker is disabled.
Required keys: user_id, fi_type, account_id.
Optional: last_transaction_date, last_ledger_date, last_cashflow_date (ISO strings),
          transaction_status, ledger_status, cashflow_status, account_display_name.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: apply_account_display_name_update
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Set account_display_name only if row exists and field is empty. Replaces enqueue_account_display_name_update.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: apply_account_display_names_for_fip_accounts
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Set account_display_name for each item directly in DB; only if the row exists and field is empty.
Each item must have .fi_data_id and .fip_name. Replaces enqueue_account_display_names_for_fip_accounts.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: AccountProcessingStatusService
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Service for account event checklist and derived flow status.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _derive_flow_status
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Derive flow_status and human-readable label from a checklist row.
Returns (flow_status_key, flow_status_label).

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _fi_type_user_facing
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Return user-facing fi_type: DEPOSIT is shown as CASH.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _last_sync_epoch_and_date
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Derive last sync epoch and datetime string from the most recent successful event.
Prefers last successful event timestamps (raw, analytics, historical_fetch, historical_insights
when status indicates success). If last sync would equal updated_at (e.g. from a failure or
processing update), uses the last successful sync date instead when available.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _row_to_account_dto
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Map a checklist row to the API account DTO (checklist + flow_status + failure + processing dates/status).

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: apply_consent_to_checklist
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
If payload is consent (ACTIVE/REJECTED with accounts[]), update checklist and return True.
Otherwise return False.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: apply_event_to_checklist
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Update checklist for this FI webhook (historical, RAW, historical_insights, analytics).
Returns True if caller should skip running the asset handler (e.g. RAW non-SUCCESS).

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: record_checklist_failure
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Update checklist last_failed_at and last_failure_reason for this account.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: get_account_processing_status
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Load checklist rows for the user and return the full detailed API-shaped payload.
Used by the test API. Returns {"accounts": [ {...full checklist, flow_status, etc...}, ... ]}.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: _row_to_user_facing_dto
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring: Map a checklist row to the simplified user-facing DTO: asset type, last sync, failure, transaction/ledger status.

app/service/account_processing_status_se.name: get_account_processing_status_user_facing
app/service/account_processing_status_se.docstring:
Load checklist rows and return a simplified user-facing payload: asset types processed,
last sync date, whether events failed, and transaction/ledger generation status.
Returns {"accounts": [ { fi_type, account_display_name, last_sync_date, events_failed, ... }, ... ]}.

app/service/analysis_allocation_service..name: get_consolidated_sector_allocation
app/service/analysis_allocation_service..docstring:
Orchestrates fetching sector data from Equity, MF, and ETF sources
and aggregates them into a consolidated view.
Resolves target user IDs internally.

app/service/analysis_allocation_service..name: get_geography_allocation
app/service/analysis_allocation_service..docstring:
Get geography allocation based on MF and ETF holdings.
Logic:
1. Resolve user scope (member/family).
2. Fetch all MF and ETF holdings for the users.
3. Identify 'Global' funds via Master data.
4. Parse 'Country Specific' from subcategory.
5. Aggregate values.

app/service/analysis_allocation_service..name: get_allocation_comparison
app/service/analysis_allocation_service..docstring:
Compare current allocation against user targets.
Supports ASSET_CLASS and SECTOR categories.

Analysis Overview Service

app/service/analysis_overview.py::Analys.name: get_aggregated_dividends
app/service/analysis_overview.py::Analys.docstring: Fetch aggregated dividend history for the user/family.

app/service/analysis_service.py.name: analysis_service.py
app/service/analysis_service.py.docstring: Analysis service layer - contains business logic for analysis calculations.

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _resolve_volatile_asset_filters
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Resolve volatile asset filters (equity, mf, etf) for metrics calculations.
Reusable helper for both key_metrics and risk_metrics.

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _calculate_date_range
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Calculate date range for metrics (1 year period ending on end_date).

Args:
    end_date: Optional end date (defaults to today)

Returns:
    Tuple of (start_date, end_date)

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _handle_parallel_exceptions
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Handle exceptions from parallel asyncio.gather execution.

Args:
    *results_with_names: Variable number of tuples (result, name) where result is the return value
                         and name is a descriptive name for logging

Returns:
    List of results with None replacing any Exception values

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: get_key_metrics
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Calculate key financial metrics: Alpha, Beta, Sharpe Ratio, Max Drawdown.
Always calculates metrics for a 1-year period ending on the specified end date.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to fetch metrics for
    family_id: Optional family ID to aggregate metrics for
    end_date: Optional end date for calculation (defaults to current date)
             Start date is always set to 1 year before this date
    asset_class_id: Optional asset class ID or Group ID to filter by
    entity_type: Optional entity type - 'asset' for asset class, 'group' for group filter

Returns:
    List of key metrics dictionaries with keys: key, value, changeType, title

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _fetch_portfolio_returns
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Fetch and calculate portfolio returns for the specified date range.

Optimized to use pandas DataFrame operations directly instead of manual loops.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of user IDs to fetch returns for
    start_date: Start date for returns
    end_date: End date for returns
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (only equity, mf, etf supported)
    equity_account_ids: Optional list of demat account IDs to filter equity by
    mf_account_ids: Optional list of MF account IDs to filter MF by
    etf_account_ids: Optional list of ETF account IDs to filter ETF by

Returns:
    pandas Series of portfolio returns as decimal (e.g., 0.01 for 1%), or None if insufficient data

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _get_benchmark_daily_returns
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Fetch daily benchmark returns for the specified date range from database.

Optimized to use pandas DataFrame operations directly instead of manual loops.

Args:
    db: Database session
    start_date: Start date for returns
    end_date: End date for returns
    benchmark_id: Optional benchmark ID (defaults to DEFAULT_BENCHMARK_ID)

Returns:
    pandas Series with date index and daily returns as decimal (e.g., 0.005 for 0.5%),
    or None if unavailable or insufficient data

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: get_risk_metrics
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Calculate risk metrics: Volatility, Max Drawdown, Sharpe Ratio, Sortino Ratio, Beta.
Always calculates metrics for a 1-year period ending on the specified end date.
Returns both portfolio and benchmark values for comparison.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to fetch metrics for
    family_id: Optional family ID to aggregate metrics for
    end_date: Optional end date for calculation (defaults to current date)
             Start date is always set to 1 year before this date
    asset_class_id: Optional asset class ID or Group ID to filter by
    entity_type: Optional entity type - 'asset' for asset class, 'group' for group filter

Returns:
    List of risk metrics dictionaries with keys: metric, portfolioValue, benchmarkValue

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _has_sufficient_data
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring: Check if series has sufficient data points for calculation.

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _calculate_risk_metrics
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Calculate all risk metrics from portfolio and benchmark returns.

Args:
    portfolio_returns: Portfolio returns as pd.Series (decimal format)
    benchmark_returns: Benchmark returns as pd.Series (decimal format)
    portfolio_raw: Raw portfolio returns data for volatility calculation

Returns:
    List of risk metrics dictionaries

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: get_top_risk_contributors
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:

Raises:

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _aggregate_holdings_by_isin
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Aggregate holdings from multiple sources by ISIN.
For equity, we use 'name' as key since they don't have holding_isin.

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _calculate_top_contributors
app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.docstring:
Calculate top contributors (assets and sectors combined) with percentages.

Args:
    holdings: List of holdings with name, value, sector, and optional asset_type
    include_sectors: Whether to include sector aggregate in the ranking
    limit: Number of top items to return


Returns:
    List of top contributors with name, percentage, and check_passed

app/service/analysis_service.py::Analysi.name: _get_group_holdings

For volatile assets (Equity, MF, ETF): get underlying holdings

Returns:

Validate benchmark ID and get API codes.

    ValueError: If benchmark_id not found in mapping

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Fetch point-to-point return for a single day using Pulse Lab (primary) or FinFactor (fallback).

Args:
    benchmark_code_pulse_lab: Pulse Lab benchmark/scheme code
    benchmark_code_finfactor: FinFactor benchmark code
    point_1: Previous trading day (date)
    point_2: Current trading day (date)

Returns:

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Fetch daily returns for each trading day in the date range using sequential API calls.

    benchmark_code_pulse_lab: Pulse Lab benchmark/scheme code
    benchmark_code_finfactor: FinFactor benchmark code
    start_date: First missing date
    end_date: Last date to fill (inclusive)
    reference_date: Reference date to use as prev_date for the first date (usually max_date from DB)

Returns:
    Dict mapping date -> daily_return (percentage, e.g., 0.5 for 0.5%)

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: _calculate_daily_prices_from_returns
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Calculate daily prices from daily returns.

Args:
    benchmark_id: ID of the benchmark
    start_date: First missing date
    end_date: Last date to fill (inclusive)
    start_price: Price on start_date - 1 day
    daily_returns: Dict mapping date -> daily_return (percentage, e.g., 0.5 for 0.5%)

Returns:
    List of BenchmarkPriceHistoryCreate objects

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: _get_latest_data_or_error
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Get latest price and date for benchmark, or return error response.

    benchmark_id: Benchmark ID

Returns:
    Tuple of (max_date, start_price)

Raises:
    ValueError: If no data exists

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: fill_missing_data_from_max_date
