Fill missing benchmark price history data from max_date + 1 to end_date (or today).

Args:
    db: Database session
    benchmark_id: ID of the benchmark
    end_date: Last date to fill (default: today - 1 day)

Returns:
    Dict containing fill results and statistics

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: _get_previous_trading_day_and_price
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Get previous trading day and its price for calculating target_date price.

Args:
    db: Database session
    benchmark_id: Benchmark ID
    max_date: Latest date in database
    latest_price: Latest price in database
    target_date: Target date to calculate price for

Returns:
    Tuple of (prev_date, prev_price)

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: prepare_previous_day_update
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Prepare context for previous day update (DB reads only).
Call API and persist in separate steps to avoid holding session during API calls.

Returns:
    Dict with codes, prev_date, prev_price, max_date; or None if already exists

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: persist_previous_day_update
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring: Persist benchmark price for target_date. Call after API fetch (no session held).

app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.name: update_previous_day_data
app/service/benchmark_price_history_serv.docstring:
Update benchmark price history for a single day (previous day).
This method is designed for daily scheduler runs.
NOTE: Holds session during API call. For parallel-safe usage, use
prepare_previous_day_update + fetch API + persist_previous_day_update with separate sessions.

Args:
    db: Database session
    benchmark_id: ID of the benchmark
    target_date: Date to update (should be previous day)

Returns:
    Dict containing update results and statistics

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Retria.name: RetriableWebhookError
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Retria.docstring: Exception raised when a webhook event should be retried

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _resolve_user_from_webhook
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring:
Resolve and validate user from webhook uniqueIdentifier.

- In all environments, we expect FinFactor to send the internal identifier
  in the form "CREST-<user_id>" (optionally with a development suffix).
- For robustness during migration, if the identifier cannot be parsed as
  a CREST id, we fall back to treating it as a raw phone number.

Returns (user_id, phone_number) if we should process; None if we should
skip (caller must return).

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _get_user_id_for_webhook
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring:
Resolve user_id from phone_number for checklist updates.
Returns 0 for deleted/unregistered users (cached with short TTL).
Uses Redis cache to avoid a DB lookup on every webhook.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _store_webhook_event
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring: Persist webhook to DB. Raises on failure.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _handle_consent_webhook
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring: If payload is consent (ACTIVE/REJECTED with accounts[]), update checklist and return True.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _normalize_historical_payload
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring: Set payload dataType from status for historical fetch events. Raises RetriableWebhookError if accountId missing.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _update_fi_checklist_for_event
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring: Update checklist for this FI webhook. Returns True if caller should skip running the handler.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: _record_handler_failure
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring: Record handler failure in checklist (via service) and in failed_events table.

app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.name: process_payload
app/service/callback_handlers.py::Callba.docstring:
Process one webhook payload. Aligns with ACCOUNT_PROCESSING_STATUS_PLAN §4.0.1 Step 5:
store event → resolve user_id → consent (3a) or FI checklist (3b–3e) → duplicate check → handler.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py.name: chat_service.py

Chat service — the main orchestrator for the CREST AI chatbot.
Handles the streaming conversation loop: LLM → tools → LLM → response.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py::_trunc.name: _truncate_tool_result
app/service/chat/chat_service.py::_trunc.docstring:
If a tool result serializes to more than MAX_TOOL_RESULT_CHARS,
truncate list items and add a hint so the LLM knows data was trimmed.
Tries to keep as many items as possible within the limit.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.name: ChatService
app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.docstring: Orchestrates the chat conversation flow with tool-calling loop.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.docstring:
Ensure we have a valid conversation_id.
Creates a new conversation if none provided or if the given ID doesn't exist.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.name: check_rate_limit
app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.docstring: Check whether the user is within the chat rate limit.

app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.name: stream_chat_response
app/service/chat/chat_service.py::ChatSe.docstring:
Stream a chat response as SSE events.

Flow:
1. Load conversation history from Redis
2. Build messages array with history + new user message
3. Stream from LLM with tool-calling loop (tools run in parallel per round)
4. Save final response to Redis

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.name: conversation_manager.py

Conversation manager for the CREST AI chatbot.
Handles conversation CRUD operations using Redis for ephemeral storage.

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.docstring: Create a new conversation and return its ID.

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.docstring:
Get messages for a conversation, applying a sliding window to keep
only the most recent messages. This prevents unbounded context growth
when sending history to the LLM.

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.name: add_message
app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.docstring: Add a message to a conversation.

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.name: list_conversations
app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.docstring: List user's conversations, most recent first.

app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.name: check_rate_limit
app/service/chat/conversation_manager.py.docstring:
Check if user is within rate limit. Returns True if allowed.
Uses INCR + EXPIRE (non-transactional pipeline for cluster compatibility).

app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py.name: crest_ai_services.py
app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py.docstring:
Dedicated business logic for CREST AI chatbot tools.

This module contains service methods specifically built for the AI chatbot.
It queries existing models/CRUDs and formats data for LLM consumption.

Separation rationale:
  - Existing services (e.g., term_deposits.py) handle data sync/ingestion from aggregators.
  - This module provides READ-ONLY query methods optimized for chatbot tool calls.
  - Keeps chatbot-specific logic isolated from core platform business logic.
  - DB queries are delegated to app/crud/crest_ai_crud.py; this layer handles
    formatting, business logic, and orchestration.

app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.name: CrestAIServices
app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.docstring: Read-only query methods for CREST AI chatbot tools.

app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.name: get_market_cap_allocation
app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.docstring:
Get user's portfolio allocation by market cap category.

Classification tiers:
  1. Equities: actual market_cap from equity_price_history (latest date).
  2. MF/ETF with explicit sub-category (Large/Mid/Small Cap, Debt, Commodity).
  3. Remaining MF/ETF: underlying holdings from mf_fund_holdings /
     etf_holdings_fund → securities_master → equity_price_history market_cap.
  4. Fallback: scheme_name keyword matching.

app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.name: get_market_cap_comparison

Compare Large/Mid/Small Cap market performance against user's cap exposure.

app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.name: _classify_market_cap
app/service/chat/crest_ai_services.py::C.docstring: Classify raw market cap (INR) into Large/Mid/Small Cap.

app/service/chat/router.py.name: router.py
app/service/chat/router.py.docstring:
Intent router for the CREST AI chatbot.

Uses a fast LLM call to classify user messages into capabilities,
then builds a focused tool set and custom system prompt for the main LLM.
The router provider/model is configurable via settings (defaults to gpt-4o-mini).

app/service/chat/router.py::_keyword_fal.name: _keyword_fallback
app/service/chat/router.py::_keyword_fal.docstring:
Fast keyword-based intent classification when the router LLM is unavailable.
Returns matched capabilities or DEFAULT_FALLBACK if no keywords match.

app/service/chat/router.py::_build_capab.name: _build_capabilities_section
app/service/chat/router.py::_build_capab.docstring:
Auto-generate the 'What You Can Do' section from CAPABILITIES.
Only includes capabilities that have a user_facing_summary set.
This keeps the system prompt in sync with the router — no manual updates needed.

app/service/chat/router.py::_build_route.name: _build_router_prompt
app/service/chat/router.py::_build_route.docstring: Build the system instruction for the router LLM.

app/service/chat/router.py::_build_histo.name: _build_history_messages
app/service/chat/router.py::_build_histo.docstring: Extract last 3 messages from history for router context.

app/service/chat/router.py::_parse_capab.name: _parse_capabilities
app/service/chat/router.py::_parse_capab.docstring: Parse capability names from LLM response (JSON or regex fallback).

app/service/chat/router.py::_router_cach.name: _router_cache_key
app/service/chat/router.py::_router_cach.docstring: Build a deterministic Redis key for router intent caching.

app/service/chat/router.py::_last_route_.name: _last_route_cache_key
app/service/chat/router.py::_last_route_.docstring: Redis key for the previous route in a conversation (for follow-up context).

app/service/chat/router.py::classify_int.name: classify_intent
app/service/chat/router.py::classify_int.docstring:
Three-tier intent classification — cache first, LLM second, keywords as safety net:
0. Redis cache (same message = same route, saves ~300ms LLM call)
1. LLM routing (primary) — understands context, handles ambiguity
2. Keyword fallback ONLY when LLM times out or errors

Returns a list like ["PORTFOLIO_OVERVIEW", "FAMILY"].

app/service/chat/router.py::build_agent_.name: build_agent_context
app/service/chat/router.py::build_agent_.docstring:
Classify intent → pick relevant tools → build custom system prompt.
Returns: (tools_list, system_prompt)

app/service/chat/router.py::CapabilityDe.name: CapabilityDefinition
app/service/chat/router.py::CapabilityDe.docstring: Maps a high-level user intent to its tools and prompt instructions.

app/service/chat/search_service.py.name: search_service.py
app/service/chat/search_service.py.docstring:
Internet search service for CREST AI chatbot.

Uses Tavily Search API to fetch real-time web results for questions
the agent cannot answer from portfolio data alone (news, events, concepts, etc.).

app/service/chat/search_service.py::Sear.name: SearchService
app/service/chat/search_service.py::Sear.docstring: Async wrapper around Tavily Search API.

app/service/chat/search_service.py::Sear.name: search

app/service/chat/system_prompt.py.name: system_prompt.py
app/service/chat/system_prompt.py.docstring:
System prompt for the CREST AI chatbot.
Defines the assistant's personality, capabilities, and behavioral rules.

Optimized for:
- Fewer tokens (faster LLM processing, lower cost)
- Clearer structure (LLM follows rules more reliably)
- Aggressive single-round tool calling (fewer round-trips = lower latency)

app/service/chat/tool_cache.py.name: tool_cache.py
app/service/chat/tool_cache.py.docstring:
Redis-backed tool result cache for the CREST AI chatbot.
Caches expensive tool results (portfolio, holdings, risk, etc.) across requests
so repeated questions within a short window don't hit the database again.

Cache keys are scoped to user_id + tool_name + sorted input params.
TTL is controlled by settings.CHAT_TOOL_CACHE_TTL_SECONDS (default 600s / 10min).

app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::_build_c.name: _build_cache_key
app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::_build_c.docstring: Build a deterministic Redis key from user_id, tool, and sorted input.

app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::get_cach.name: get_cached_result
app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::get_cach.docstring: Retrieve a cached tool result from Redis. Returns None on miss.

app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::set_cach.name: set_cached_result
app/service/chat/tool_cache.py::set_cach.docstring: Store a successful tool result in Redis with TTL.

app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py.name: tool_definitions.py
app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py.docstring:
Tool definitions for the CREST AI chatbot.
Each tool maps to an existing backend service method.
Descriptions guide the LLM on when to call each tool.

CONVENTIONS:
- Field naming conventions are documented once in the system prompt (not repeated per tool).
- Most tools accept optional target_user_id to query a family member's data.
- Family-aggregate tools (get_family_*) always operate on the entire family.

app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py::_b.name: _build_tool_definitions
app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py::_b.docstring: Build the full list of tool definitions (called once at import time).

app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py::ge.name: get_tool_definitions
app/service/chat/tool_definitions.py::ge.docstring: Return all available tool definitions (pre-built at import time).

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py.name: tool_executor.py
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py.docstring:
Tool executor for the CREST AI chatbot.
Bridges LLM tool calls to existing CREST backend services.
Handles data isolation via user_id and formats results for LLM consumption.

Architecture (7-Pillar Resilience):
  - Pillar 1: Smart Input Resolution — fuzzy matching for names → IDs and names → ISINs
  - Pillar 2: Validation Guardrails — pre-flight checks before calling backend services
  - Pillar 3: Tool Chaining — log when LLM skips prerequisite tools
  - Pillar 4: Rich Error Messages — structured, actionable errors for the LLM
  - Pillar 5: Response Quality Layer — meta hints in tool outputs
  - Pillar 6: Session Context — cache family_map and ISINs across tool calls

Security: Every tool that accepts target_user_id verifies the target is in
the caller's family group before executing. The LLM cannot access arbitrary users.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_safe.name: _safe_float
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_safe.docstring: Convert a value to float, handling formatted strings like '₹2.70 L' or '17.5%'.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_trim.name: _trim_list
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_trim.docstring:
Sort + cap a list. Returns (trimmed_list, note_or_None).
Avoids repeating the same trim-sort-note pattern across every tool.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_inje.name: _inject_absolute_gain
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_inje.docstring: Add absolute_gain_inr (metric_value - costBasis) to each holding dict.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_fuzz.name: _fuzzy_match_name
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::_fuzz.docstring:
Case-insensitive fuzzy matching of a query string against a list of dicts.
Tries exact match → starts-with → substring. Returns best match or None.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: ToolExecutor
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Executes tool calls by delegating to existing CREST services.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: execute
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Execute a tool by name with the given input.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: execute_parallel
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Execute a tool with its own DB session — safe for asyncio.gather().

When multiple tools run concurrently, each needs an independent DB session
(SQLAlchemy AsyncSession is not safe for concurrent queries on the same session).
This method creates a temporary ToolExecutor with its own session for the call.
If no session_factory is available, falls back to the shared session.

Tools in _TOOLS_WITH_INTERNAL_CONCURRENCY use asyncio.gather() inside their
service layer. A fresh session's first query triggers connection provisioning,
and the internal concurrent queries race against that → crash. These tools get
a pre-warmed session (SELECT 1 first) so the connection is established before
the service layer's asyncio.gather runs.

IMPORTANT: session_factory must be an async_sessionmaker (callable that returns
AsyncSession directly), NOT an async generator like get_async_session.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _execute_with_own_session
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Run a tool with a fresh DB session (called under semaphore).

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _execute_with_warm_session
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Run a tool that has internal asyncio.gather with a pre-warmed session.

These tools (e.g. get_sector_allocation) use asyncio.gather() inside
their service layer to run multiple queries concurrently on the session
they receive. A cold session crashes because connection provisioning
can't handle concurrent operations. By running SELECT 1 first, we
establish the DB connection so the internal gather works.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _check_prerequisites
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Log when LLM skips prerequisite tools (non-blocking — Pillar 1 handles resolution).

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_handler
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Map tool name to handler method (uses pre-built dict from __init__).

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _serialize
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Recursively serialize data for JSON, handling dates, decimals, etc.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_family_member_ids
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Get the set of user_ids that the current user can access (their family).
Handles both family owners AND family members.
Cached after first call per request.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _resolve_target_user_id
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Resolve and validate target_user_id from tool params.

Pillar 1 (Smart Input Resolution):
  - Integer → validate directly against family members
  - String name (e.g. "Neha") → fuzzy match against family member names
  - Relationship (e.g. "wife") → match against relationship field
  - Not provided → return self.user_id

Raises ValueError with helpful message if name can't be resolved.
Raises PermissionError if target is not in caller's family.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_family_members_data
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Pillar 6: Get full family member dicts (cached per request).
Used by Pillar 1's fuzzy matching and avoids redundant DB calls.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_cached_stress_options
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Pillar 6: Get stress test options (cached per request and per target user).
Used by Pillar 1's ISIN resolution.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _resolve_isin
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Pillar 1: Resolve a stress test factor value to a valid ISIN.

If value looks like an ISIN (INE*/INF*) → pass through.
If value is a name string → fuzzy match against stress test options.

Returns: (resolved_isin, resolved_name) or raises ValueError.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _resolve_member_uid
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Common pattern: resolve target_user_id and compute the member_user_id
argument for services that use (requester_user_id, member_user_id).

Returns: (target_uid, member_uid)
  - member_uid is None when querying own data, or the target's uid for family queries.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _call_service_for_user
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Shorthand for: resolve target → call service(requester, member) → serialize → attach user_id.
`service_call` receives (requester_user_id, member_user_id) and returns raw result.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _resolve_asset_class_filter
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring:
Resolve an optional asset_class string param (e.g. 'equity', 'mf')
to (filter_id, entity_type) for the holdings service.
Returns (None, None) if no filter requested.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _calculate
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Evaluate a mathematical expression with AST-based guardrails.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _clean_harvesting_opportunity
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Keep only LLM-relevant fields from a tax harvesting opportunity.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _call_family_service
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Common pattern for family aggregate tools.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _resolve_family_id
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Resolve the family_id for the current user (owner or member).

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_fd_details
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get Fixed Deposit (Term Deposit) details.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_rd_details

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get NPS (National Pension Scheme) details with tier breakdown.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_goal_progress
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Compare current allocation against user-set targets.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_fund_holdings
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get underlying holdings of a specific mutual fund.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get top/bottom performing equities across the entire market.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_top_fund_performers
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get top/bottom performing MFs/ETFs across the entire market.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_sector_market_comparison
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Compare user's sector allocation against market-wide sector performance.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_market_cap_allocation
app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Get user's portfolio allocation by market cap category.

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.name: _get_market_cap_comparison

app/service/chat/tool_executor.py::ToolE.docstring: Search the internet via Tavily for real-time information.

app/service/corporate_actions.py.name: corporate_actions.py
app/service/corporate_actions.py.docstring:
Corporate Actions Service

Business logic for fetching and saving corporate actions data from Morningstar API.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Simple.name: SimpleRateLimiter
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Simple.docstring:
Allow `rate` acquisitions per `per` seconds.
All API calls go through acquire() so QPS is enforced centrally.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: CorporateActionsService
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Service layer for corporate actions operations.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: build_corporate_actions_url
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Build Morningstar corporate actions API URL.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: _parse_dividend_amount
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Extract dividend amount from Corporate Action - D field(s).

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: parse_event_data
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Parse corporate action event data from API response.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: parse_json
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Parse JSON and handle duplicate 'Corporate Action - D' keys only.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: fetch_corporate_actions
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Fetch corporate actions from Morningstar API for a symbol.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: _get_security_from_context
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring:
Extract security info from SecurityContext.
Returns dict with stock_id and symbol for compatibility.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: _fetch_and_save_single
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Fetch and save corporate actions for a single security using SecurityContext.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: _worker
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring: Worker to process securities from the queue.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: bulk_fetch_and_save
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring:
Bulk fetch and save corporate actions for multiple securities using SecurityContext.

Optimizations:
- Uses SecurityContext for consistent security identifiers
- Worker pool pattern for controlled concurrency
- Rate limiting at 50 req/sec
- Parallel DB writes with semaphore control

NOTE: This method expects a list of `SecurityContext` objects which are
pre-fetched from the database. It does NOT query the securities_master
table again, ensuring efficient "Fetch Once" behavior.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: sync_corporate_actions
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring:
Sync corporate actions: historical (all data) or daily (since yesterday).
NOTE: Call sync_corporate_actions_from_engine from scheduler to avoid holding
session during long API/DB work. This method remains for backward compatibility.

app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.name: sync_corporate_actions_from_engine
app/service/corporate_actions.py::Corpor.docstring:
Sync corporate actions using engine. Fetches security_contexts in short session,
then bulk_fetch with no parent session held during API calls.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: CostBasisEngine
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Dedicated engine for calculating cost basis independently of TaxOptimisationService.
Implements FIFO logic for Equities and Weighted Average for MF/ETFs.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _should_exclude_duplicate
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring: Exclude pledge/eDIS from cost basis calculation

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _is_demerger_origin_stock
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Detect if this is a child company that originated from a demerger.

Child companies (received via demerger) should KEEP the demerger transaction
and use the transaction rate as cost basis.

Parent companies (that underwent demerger) should FILTER the demerger transaction
and apply cost apportionment via corporate_actions.

Detection: Returns True if earliest BUY transaction has 'DEMERGER' in narration.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _is_corporate_action_txn
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Check if a transaction should be IGNORED for cost basis calculations.

Filter out:
1. Corporate Actions (BONUS, SPLIT, etc.) - handled via corporate_actions table
2. BY PLEDGE - duplicate BUY entry when shares are pledged
3. BY EDIS - duplicate BUY entry when shares are blocked for trading

Args:
    txn: The transaction to check
    is_demerger_origin: If True, this stock is a child company from demerger.
                        DEMERGER transactions will NOT be filtered.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _filter_simultaneous_buy_sell
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Filter out "Pledge Pairs" using pairwise filtering logic.

Logic:
1. Identify candidates: Transactions with "PLEDGE" or "BLOCK MECHANISM" in narration.
2. Group candidates by (date, units, rate).
3. For each group:
   - If exactly 1 BUY and 1 SELL -> It's a system pair. Remove both.
   - Else -> Keep all (unpaired pledges or complex scenarios).
4. Keep all non-candidates.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _preprocess_equity_transactions
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Clean transactions for Equity calculations.

Includes smart demerger handling:
- Child companies (demerger-origin): Keep demerger BUY, use transaction rate
- Parent companies: Filter demerger txn, apply CA apportionment

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: calculate_equity_cost_basis
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Calculates Equity Cost Basis using FIFO method + Corporate Actions.
Uses EquityPriceHistory close_price.
Logic: Looks for exact date match. If missing, finds the nearest PRIOR date.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _reconstruct_equity_lots
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring:
Reconstruct FIFO lots from transactions and corporate actions.
Returns list of lots.

Args:
    all_demerger_cas: Optional list of ALL demerger CAs around the transaction date range.
                      Used to find parent company's child_instrument_price for child stocks.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: _calculate_weighted_average
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring: Calculates the total cost basis for the currently held units using a weighted average approach.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: calculate_mf_cost_basis
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring: Calculates MF Cost Basis using Weighted Average method.

app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.name: calculate_etf_cost_basis
app/service/cost_basis_engine.py::CostBa.docstring: Calculates ETF Cost Basis using Weighted Average method.

app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.name: DailyCashFlowsService
app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.docstring:
Service for populating and managing daily cash flows.

Uses server-side aggregation (fast and efficient) for processing transactions.
Simple, maintainable, production-ready.

app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.name: bulk_upsert_cash_flows
app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.docstring:
Bulk upsert daily cash flows in batches to handle large datasets efficiently.

PRODUCTION OPTIMIZATIONS:
1. Single-pass processing (more memory efficient)
2. Batched commits to reduce transaction overhead
3. Uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT for efficient upserts
4. Proper error handling and rollback on failure

Args:
    db: Database session
    cash_flows: List of dicts with keys: user_id, asset_class_id, demat_id, flow_date, cash_flow_amount
    batch_size: Number of records to process per batch (default: 5000)

Returns:
    Number of rows inserted/updated

Raises:
    Exception: If batch processing fails, transaction is rolled back

app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.name: populate_equity_cash_flows
app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.docstring:
Populate cash flows for equity transactions. When account_id_for_sync is set,
enqueues cashflow_status=generating at start and cashflow_status=success
+ last_cashflow_date + last_ledger_date at end.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    start_date: Start date of the period
    end_date: End date of the period
    account_id: Optional account ID to filter by (if provided, only processes transactions for this account)
    account_id_for_sync: fi_data_id (payload account id) for fi_account_sync_status events

app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.name: populate_mf_cash_flows
app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.docstring:
Populate cash flows for MF transactions.
When mf_account_id is provided, only that FI account's transactions are aggregated
and rows are stored with mf_account_id so Account B does not overwrite Account A.
When mf_account_id is set, enqueues cashflow_status=generating at start
and cashflow_status=success + last_cashflow_date + last_ledger_date at end.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    start_date: Start date of the period
    end_date: End date of the period
    mf_account_id: Optional FI account id from webhook (accountId); when set, scope to that account

app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.name: populate_etf_cash_flows
app/service/daily_cash_flows.py::DailyCa.docstring:
Populate cash flows for ETF transactions.
When etf_account_id is provided, only that ETF account's transactions are aggregated
and rows are stored with etf_account_id so Account B does not overwrite Account A.
When account_id_for_sync is set, enqueues cashflow_status=generating at start
and cashflow_status=success + last_cashflow_date + last_ledger_date at end.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    start_date: Start date of the period
    end_date: End date of the period
    etf_account_id: Optional ETF account id (etf_accounts.id); when set, scope to that account
    account_id_for_sync: Payload accountId for fi_account_sync_status events

app/service/dashboard_service.py.name: dashboard_service.py
app/service/dashboard_service.py.docstring: Dashboard service layer - contains business logic for dashboard/portfolio calculations.

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: DashboardService
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring: Service layer for dashboard and portfolio calculations

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _fetch_balances
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Helper to fetch balances for a specific date using a dedicated session.
Avoids concurrent operation conflicts by using separate sessions.

Args:
    db: Database session (used to get bind for new session)
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    as_of_date: Optional date to fetch balances as of (None for current)
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (e.g., "equity", "deposits")
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs to filter by (e.g., demat_id, mf_id)
    **kwargs: Additional filter arguments

Returns:
    Dictionary with balance data

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _fetch_earliest_balances
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Helper to fetch earliest available balances using a dedicated session.

Args:
    db: Database session (used to get bind for new session)
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type to filter by (e.g., "equity", "deposits")
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs to filter by (e.g., demat_id, mf_id)
    **kwargs: Additional filter arguments

Returns:
    Tuple of (Dictionary with earliest balance data, minimum date)

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _calculate_since_inception_xirr
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Calculate XIRR for 'since inception' metric based on filters.

XIRR is calculated only for:
- Combined equity+ETF demat filter - equity demat-specific XIRR (best available)
- EQUITY (with demat filter) - demat-specific XIRR
- EQUITY (without demat filter) - equity asset class XIRR
- MF - mutual fund asset class XIRR
- ETF - ETF asset class XIRR

For any other asset class or no asset class filter, returns None
(which triggers absolute gain calculation as fallback).

Uses a dedicated session to avoid concurrent operation conflicts.

Args:
    db: Database session (used to get bind for new session)
    target_user_ids: List of user IDs
    asset_class_type: Optional asset class type filter
    user_asset_ids: Optional list of user asset IDs (e.g., demat_id)
    end_date: End date for calculation
    start_date: Optional start date for period XIRR
    extra_kwargs: Optional dict with equity_account_ids/etf_account_ids for combined demat filter

Returns:
    XIRR value as percentage, or None if calculation fails or not applicable

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _get_asset_class_filter_details
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get asset class type and user asset IDs for filtering when asset_class_id is provided.

Logic based on AssetClasses configuration:
- If AssetClass.is_generic=True: Broad filter (Equity, MF, etc.) -> Returns generic type, no specific user assets.
- If AssetClass.is_generic=False: Specific filter (Zerodha, Groww) -> Returns type, plus specific user assets linked to this class.
- For non-generic EQUITY classes: Also resolves ETF accounts from the same physical demat account
  and returns them as extra_kwargs (mirroring the group filter pattern).

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    asset_class_id: Optional asset class ID to filter by

Returns:
    Tuple of (asset_class_type, user_asset_ids, extra_kwargs):
    - asset_class_type: The AssetClassType enum value (None for combined equity+ETF)
    - user_asset_ids: List of specific user_asset_ids (if specific filter) or None (if generic filter)
    - extra_kwargs: Dict of additional filter args (equity_account_ids, etf_account_ids) for combined filters

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _resolve_group_assets
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Resolve a Group ID into lists of specific asset IDs for filtering.

Args:
    db: Database session
    group_id: Asset Group ID
    target_user_ids: User IDs (for ownership check)

Returns:
    Dict containing specific asset ID lists:
    {
        "equity_account_ids": [1, 2],
        "mf_account_ids": [3],
        "manual_asset_ids": [10, 11]
        # ... etc
    }

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: calculate_current_wealth
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Calculate current total wealth by summing all types of investments.

Currently includes:
- Deposits
- Recurring Deposits
- Term Deposits
- NPS
- Equity (demat holdings)
- ETFs
_fetch_balances
Future investment types to be added:
- Mutual Funds (MF)
- Exchange Traded Funds (ETF)
- National Pension Scheme (NPS)
- Other asset types

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs to calculate wealth for

Returns:
    Dictionary containing total wealth and individual balance components

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_total_wealth_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get total wealth summary with year-over-year change.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    current_wealth: Current wealth value

Returns:
    PortfolioSummaryItem with value, change, changeType, subtitle, title, is_CTA_Available

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_ytd_return_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get YTD return summary.
Calculates return from April 1st of current year to now.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    current_wealth: Current wealth value

Returns:
    PortfolioSummaryItem with value, change, changeType, subtitle, title, is_CTA_Available

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_volatility_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get volatility summary.
Calculates annualized portfolio volatility from equity_ledger, mf_ledger, and etf_ledger
for the last 365 days using value-weighted portfolio returns.

Process:
1. Fetches daily portfolio returns (value-weighted, market movements only) for last 365 days
2. Calculates standard deviation of daily returns (sample std, n-1)
3. Annualizes by multiplying by √252

Returns are based on market price/NAV changes only, excluding buy/sell transactions.
Filters extreme values outside -30% to +50% range.
Requires minimum 20 days of data.

Uses caching to avoid expensive recalculations.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs

Returns:
    PortfolioSummaryItem with value, change, changeType, subtitle, title, is_CTA_Available

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_cash_position_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get cash position summary.
Calculates cash percentage as: (deposits_now + liquid_mf) / total_wealth) * 100

Args:
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    current_wealth_data: Dictionary containing current wealth breakdown
    liquid_mf_balance: Pre-fetched liquid mutual funds balance

Returns:
    PortfolioSummaryItem with value, change, changeType, subtitle, title, is_CTA_Available

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_holdings_count_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get per-asset-type holdings count as KPI cards.
Returns 3 cards: Equity Holdings, MF Holdings, ETF Holdings.
Each count includes manual assets of that respective type.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs

Returns:
    List of 3 PortfolioSummaryItem cards (Equity, MF, ETF)

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_optimization_opportunities_as_summary_items
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get optimization opportunities as OptimizationOpportunityItem list.
Calculates idle cash optimization from real data and includes mock data for other opportunities.

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    deposits_now: Optional current deposits value to reuse
    total_portfolio_value: Optional total portfolio value for percentage calculation

Returns:
    List of OptimizationOpportunityItem objects representing optimization opportunities

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_potential_savings_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get potential savings summary.
Calculates as: deposits_now * expected_return

Args:
    db: Database session
    target_user_ids: List of target user IDs
    deposits_now: Optional current deposits value to reuse (avoids duplicate query)

Returns:
    PortfolioSummaryItem with value, change, changeType, subtitle, title, is_CTA_Available

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _get_benchmark_return
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Fetch benchmark returns for the configured benchmark between two dates.
Uses Redis caching to avoid redundant API calls for the same date range.
Cache expires early morning next day (2 AM) to ensure fresh data daily.
The same cached value is shared across all users for the same date range.

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: _get_aggregated_tax_optimisation
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Fetch and aggregate tax optimisation data for all target users.
Uses caching to avoid expensive recalculations.

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_portfolio_summary
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get portfolio summary data for the user.
Returns lightweight summary items only (Total Wealth, YTD Return, Cash Position).


Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user
    member_user_id: ID of the member (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family (optional)

Returns:
    Dictionary with 'summary' (List[PortfolioSummaryItem]) containing lightweight items only

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_portfolio_volatility
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get portfolio volatility with benchmark comparison (same logic as risk metrics API).

This is a heavy calculation that processes 365 days of returns data.
Uses caching for fast subsequent requests.
Also fetches benchmark (default: BSE 500) daily returns for the same period,
computes annualized benchmark volatility, and returns comparison.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: ID of the member (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family (optional)

Returns:
    Dict with PortfolioSummaryItem fields plus benchmarkVolatility, benchmarkName,
    and comparison fields (change, changeType, subtitle) matching YTD pattern.

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_portfolio_optimization_opportunities
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get optimization opportunities for the portfolio.

This is a heavy calculation that analyzes multiple optimization scenarios.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: ID of the member (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family (optional)

Returns:
    List of PortfolioSummaryItem objects with optimization opportunities

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_portfolio_potential_savings
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get potential savings and optimization opportunities for the portfolio.

Potential savings is the sum of all optimization opportunity values.
This method returns both the total potential savings and the breakdown
of optimization opportunities.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: ID of the member (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family (optional)

Returns:
    Dictionary containing:
        - potential_savings: PortfolioSummaryItem with total potential savings
        - optimization_opportunities: List[PortfolioSummaryItem] with breakdown

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_current_value
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring: Get current value metrics with optional start and end date filters.

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_performance_metrics
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get performance metrics using portfolio XIRR for different time periods (1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 3Y, 5Y).

Calculates portfolio XIRR only - no asset class filters applied.

Args:

    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to fetch metrics for
    family_id: Optional family ID to aggregate metrics for
    start_date: Optional start date filter (not used for XIRR calculation)
    end_date: Optional end date (defaults to current date if not provided)
    asset_class_id: Optional asset class ID (ignored - portfolio XIRR only)

Returns:
    List of PerformanceMetricsItem with period, value, and changeType

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_performance_chart
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get performance chart data by dividing the date range into granular points based on duration.

Fetches asset values concurrently for each date using separate db sessions.

Args:
    start_date: Start date for the performance chart (required)
    end_date: End date for the performance chart (required)
    requester_user_id: ID of the current user
    db: Database session
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to filter by
    family_id: Optional family ID to filter by

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_asset_allocation
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get asset allocation data for the authenticated user.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the requester user
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to filter by
    family_id: Optional family ID to filter by
    start_date: Optional start date for filtering balances
    end_date: Optional end date (defaults to current date)
    asset_class_id: Optional asset class ID to filter by (not currently used)

Returns:
    List of AssetAllocationItem objects

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_top_holdings
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get top holdings from deposits, term_deposits, recurring_deposits, and nps.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the requester user
    member_user_id: Optional member user ID to filter by
    family_id: Optional family ID to filter by

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_asset_types
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get asset types data for filtering purposes with family support.
Returns two lists: one for individual assets and one for asset groups.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the authenticated user
    member_user_id: ID of the member to fetch asset types for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to fetch asset types for (optional)

Returns:
    Dict containing:
    - assets: List of assets with label (user_asset_name or asset_class name)
    - groups: List of asset groups

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: calculate_regular_vs_direct_mf_savings
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Calculate potential savings by switching from regular to direct mutual funds.

Steps:
1. Fetch all mutual funds of a user
2. For regular mutual funds, fetch direct alternatives from regular_direct_mf_mapping
3. If not present in DB, fetch using get_broker_direct from pulse_lab service
4. If receive more than one obj, match schemeOption
5. Store it in DB
6. Fetch latest direct and regular nav from historic nav
7. Multiply units with their respective scheme_code and their alternative direct scheme_code navs
8. Sum up direct ones separately and regular ones separately
9. Return direct sum - regular sum

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID to calculate savings for

Returns:
    float: Potential savings (direct sum - regular sum)

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_tax_breakdown
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get tax breakdown for an individual member or aggregated for a family.

Returns net STCG, net LTCG, harvestable STCL, and harvestable LTCL.

Args:
    db: Database session
    requester_user_id: ID of the user making the request
    member_user_id: ID of the member to get breakdown for (optional)
    family_id: ID of the family to aggregate breakdown for (optional)

Returns:
    TaxBreakdownResponse with net gains and harvestable losses

app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.name: get_data_availability_date
app/service/dashboard_service.py::Dashbo.docstring:
Get the oldest and latest transaction dates for a user formatted as dd-mm-yyyy.

Returns:

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: fetch_deposits_data

Main orchestrating function to fetch and save deposits data for a user

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: _fetch_and_process_deposits_data

Fetch and process deposits data, store in DB, and return account_ids and mapping.

Returns:

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: _process_account_statements

Process account statements for multiple accounts with upsert logic

    account_to_deposit_id: Dict mapping account_id (string) to deposit_id (int)

Returns:

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: _map_statement_data
app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.docstring:
Map and validate statement data from aggregator service to database format

Args:
    statement: Raw statement data from aggregator
    user_id: User ID to associate with statement
    account_id: Account ID to associate with statement
    deposit_id: Deposit ID (primary key from deposits table) to associate with statement

Returns:

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: _create_ledger_entries_from_dataframe
app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.docstring: Convert ledger DataFrame rows to DepositsLedgerBase objects.

app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.name: generate_deposits_ledger_from_transactions
app/service/deposits.py::DepositsService.docstring:
Generate deposits ledger entries from bank statements within a date range.
Defaults to last 1 year if dates not provided.

Logic:
1. Fetch bank statements for the period
2. For each account_id and each day, use the last statement's current_balance
3. For days without statements, forward fill the last known balance
4. Calculate previous_day_value and daily_returns_percentage
5. Map account_id to deposit_id for FK constraint
6. Store in deposits_ledger table

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID to generate ledger for
    start_date: Start date of the period (defaults to 1 year ago)
    end_date: End date of the period (defaults to today)

Returns:
    Dict containing processing statistics

app/service/dividend_service.py.name: dividend_service.py
app/service/dividend_service.py.docstring:
Dividend Service

Business logic for calculating and storing user dividends based on:
1. Corporate actions (dividend events with record_date, amount)
2. Equity ledger (user holdings at record_date)

Two main workflows:
1. Backfill: On user onboarding, calculate past dividends for configured years
2. Daily: Process dividends for corporate actions with record_date = today - 3 (T+3 settlement)

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: DividendService
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Service layer for dividend operations.

Handles:
- Initial backfill for new users (past N years)
- Daily processing for dividends with record_date = today - 3

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: backfill_dividends_for_user
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Backfill dividend records for a user for the past N years.

Called after first ledger generation to populate historical dividends.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID to backfill dividends for
    years: Number of years to backfill (default from settings)

Returns:
    Dict with backfill statistics

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _get_dividend_actions_in_range
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Fetch dividend corporate actions within a date range.

Args:
    db: Database session
    start_date: Start date (inclusive)
    end_date: End date (inclusive)

Returns:
    List of CorporateAction records with event_label = 'Dividend'

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _calculate_dividends_for_user_security
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Calculate dividend records for a user for a specific security.

For each dividend action, finds user's holdings at record_date from ledger.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    security_id: Security ID
    actions: List of dividend corporate actions for this security

Returns:
    List of dividend record dictionaries

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _get_user_holdings_at_date
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Get user's holdings for a security at a specific date (per demat account).

Uses subquery to get the latest ledger entry on or before target_date
for each demat account for the specific security.

Args:
    db: Database session
    user_id: User ID
    security_id: Security ID
    target_date: Date to check holdings at

Returns:
    List of dicts with demat_id and units

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _get_company_name
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring: Get company name from securities_master for denormalization.

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: process_daily_dividends
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Process dividends for corporate actions with record_date = today - days_ago.

Called daily by the scheduler. Uses T+3 settlement logic - we process
dividends 3 days after record date to ensure ledger has accurate data.

Args:
    db: Database session
    days_ago: Number of days before today to process (default 3 for T+3)

Returns:
    Dict with processing statistics

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: process_periodic_backfill_sweep
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Run a safety sweep backfill for the last 1 year.

Runs periodically (Mondays and Fridays) to catch missed dividends/updates.
Uses action-centric approach for efficiency.

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _get_dividend_actions_for_date
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring: Get dividend actions for a specific record date.

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _process_single_dividend_action
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Process a single dividend action for all users.

Finds all users with holdings at record_date and creates dividend records.

Args:
    db: Database session
    action: CorporateAction record

Returns:
    Number of dividend records created

app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.name: _get_all_users_holdings_at_date
app/service/dividend_service.py::Dividen.docstring:
Get all users' holdings for a security at a specific date.

Used by daily job to process dividends for all eligible users.

Args:
    db: Database session
    security_id: Security ID
    target_date: Record date to check

Returns:
    List of dicts with user_id, demat_id, units

app/service/equity.py::Equity.name: Equity
app/service/equity.py::Equity.docstring:
Service class for managing equity holdings, transactions, and ledger generation.
Handles demat account data processing, transaction reconciliation, and ledger creation.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_execute_.name: _execute_parallel_db_operation

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_firs.name: _get_first_transaction_date_parallel
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_firs.docstring: Helper to get first transaction date using a separate session for parallel execution

    start_holdings: Holdings at start of current year
    is_forward_generation: True if forward generation

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_create_m.docstring:
Create monthly snapshot ledger entries for all month starts within a date range.

Args:
    starting_holdings: Holdings to use for snapshots
    account_id: Demat account ID
    start_date: Start date of the range
    end_date: End date of the range (inclusive for month starts)
    skip_months_before_start: If True, skip month starts before start_date.
                             If False, include month start if it's in the same month as start_date.

Returns:
    List of ledger entry dictionaries for monthly snapshots

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_handle_y.name: _handle_year_without_transactions
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_handle_y.docstring:
Handle year with no transactions by creating monthly snapshots if needed.

For backward generation: Creates monthly snapshots to fill historical gaps.
For forward generation: Creates monthly snapshots to maintain the monthly snapshot pattern
(e.g., if last snapshot was Dec 1, 2025 and we're generating forward to Jan 2, 2026,
we should create Jan 1, 2026 snapshot).

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    year: Year being processed
    year_start: Start date for the year
    year_end: End date for the year
    starting_holdings: Holdings to use for snapshots
    min_ledger_date: Minimum ledger date (if available)
    is_forward_generation: True if forward generation

Returns:
    Number of rows written

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_star.name: _get_starting_holdings_from_ledger
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_star.docstring:
Get starting holdings from ledger based on generation type.

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    user_id: User ID
    oldest_ledger_date: Reference ledger date (latest for forward, oldest for backward)
    is_forward_generation: True if forward generation, False if backward

Returns:
    Tuple of (holdings dict, min_ledger_date if backward generation)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_star.name: _get_starting_holdings_from_current
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_star.docstring:
Get starting holdings from current holdings (for RAW event with empty ledger).

Args:
    db: Database session

Returns:
    Holdings dictionary

app/service/equity.py::Equity::fetch_dem.name: fetch_demat_holdings_data
app/service/equity.py::Equity::fetch_dem.docstring:
Fetch demat holdings data for a user by phone number and store in database

Args:
    db: Database session
    phone_number: User's phone number
    payload: Optional webhook payload containing metadata (status, dataType, accountId)
Returns:
    Dict containing API response data and processing results

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.name: _process_and_store_demat_data
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.docstring:
Process demat data from API response and store in database.
Processes per demat account, tracking which years have been processed to avoid duplicate work.

Event Types:
- RAW: First event per demat - contains current data + 1 year transactions
- HISTORICAL_DATA_FETCH_PARTIAL_SUCCESS: Multiple events per demat - partial years (unknown)
- HISTORICAL_DATA_FETCH_SUCCESS: One event per demat - all 20 years ready

Args:
    db: Database session
    demat_data: Raw demat data from API
    user_id: User ID to associate with records
    phone_number: User's phone number
    target_account_id: Optional specific account ID to process (fiDataId)
    fetch_years: Number of years to fetch (1 for raw, 20 for historical)

Returns:
    Dict containing processing statistics

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.name: _process_demat_account
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.docstring:
Process a single demat account and store in database

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_data: Account data from API
    user_id: User ID

Returns:
    Created/updated DematAccount object

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.name: _process_demat_holdings
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.docstring:
Process demat holdings and store in database, optionally create equity ledger entries.
Uses prevDetails from API response for previous day values (optimized - no DB queries needed).

Args:
    db: Database session
    holdings_data: List of holding data from API (includes prevDetails)
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Demat account ID (integer)
    demat_id: Demat ID (string from demat_accounts.demat_id)
    build_ledger_entries: If True, build ledger entries from holdings (for 1-day updates)

Returns:
    Number of holdings processed

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_upsert_a.name: _upsert_and_map_holdings
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_upsert_a.docstring:
Process holdings data, upsert to database, and return mapping of ISIN to original data.
Uses bulk ISIN lookup via pandas for efficiency.

Args:
    db: Database session
    holdings_data: List of holding data from API
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Demat account ID

Returns:
    Tuple of (upserted_holdings, original_data_map)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_reconcil.name: _reconcile_sold_stocks
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_reconcil.docstring:
Reconcile sold stocks using single-query deletion.
Deletes holdings where ISIN is NOT in current API response.

This method uses a single DELETE query with NOT IN clause, which is more
efficient than fetching existing holdings and comparing in Python.

Logic:
1. Extract ISINs from current API response
2. Call single DELETE query with NOT IN clause
3. Database handles the comparison and deletion

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    current_holdings_data: List of holding data from current API response

Returns:
    Number of holdings deleted (sold stocks)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_an.name: _build_and_upsert_ledger_entries
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_an.docstring:
Build equity ledger entries from upserted holdings and upsert them.

Args:
    db: Database session
    upserted_holdings: List of upserted holding objects
    original_map: Mapping of ISIN to original holding data (includes prevDetails)
    user_id: User ID
    demat_id: Demat ID (string)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_eq.name: _fetch_equity_statements_for_year_range
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_eq.docstring:
Fetch equity account statements for a specific year range with timeout handling

Args:

    date_range_from: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
    date_range_to: End date (YYYY-MM-DD)

    timeout_seconds: Timeout in seconds for the API call

Returns:

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.docstring:
Process demat account statements for a specific account within a date range.
Fetches transactions and stores them in the database.

Args:
    db: Database session
    phone_number: User's phone number
    fi_data_id: Account fi_data_id for API calls
    account_id: Demat account ID (integer)
    user_id: User ID
    dateRangeFrom: Start date for fetching transactions
    end_date: End date for fetching transactions

Returns:
    (Number of transactions processed, max transaction date from in-memory data or None)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_map_stat.name: _map_statement_to_transaction
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_map_stat.docstring:
Map statement data to transaction schema.

Args:
    statement: Raw statement data from API
    user_id: User ID
    account_id: Account ID
    isin_to_security_id: Mapping of ISIN to security_id

Returns:
    Mapped transaction dict or None if invalid

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_calculat.name: _calculate_year_ranges
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_calculat.docstring:
Calculate yearly date ranges between start and end dates.

Args:
    start_date: Start date
    end_date: End date

Returns:
    List of dicts with "from" and "to" date strings

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_tr.name: _fetch_transactions_for_demat_account
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_tr.docstring:
Fetch transactions for a demat account.
When account_id_for_sync is set, enqueues transaction_status=generating at start
and transaction_status=success + last_transaction_date at end.

For initial onboarding (HISTORICAL events):
- Fetches backward: 1 year before oldest transaction date, or from config start date

For daily RAW events (incremental):
- Fetches forward: from (last transaction date + 1 day) to current date

Args:
    db: Database session
    phone_number: User's phone number
    fi_data_id: Account fi_data_id for API calls
    account_id: Demat account ID
    user_id: User ID
    end_date: End date for fetching (typically current date)
    fetch_years: Number of years to fetch (1 for RAW, 20 for HISTORICAL)
    account_id_for_sync: fi_data_id (payload account id) for fi_account_sync_status events

Returns:
    Number of transactions processed

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_generate.name: _generate_ledger_for_demat_account
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_generate.docstring:
Generate ledger for a demat account. When account_id_for_sync is set, enqueues
ledger_status=generating at start and ledger_status=success + last_ledger_date at end.

Logic based on event type:
- RAW event + empty ledger: backward generation for 1 year, use current holdings
- HISTORY event: backward generation, use holdings snapshot at min(ledger_date)
- RAW event + ledger exists: forward generation, use latest ledger date

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    user_id: User ID
    is_raw_event: True if this is a RAW event (fetch_years=1), False if HISTORY event (fetch_years=20)
    account_id_for_sync: fi_data_id (payload account id) for fi_account_sync_status events

Returns:
    (ledger_rows_generated, last_ledger_date). last_ledger_date is oldest date of generated range when rows > 0, else None.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_prepare_.name: _prepare_current_holdings_state_per_demat
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_prepare_.docstring:
Prepare current holdings state dictionary for a specific demat account.
Key is security_id only (since we're processing per demat account).

Args:
    all_stocks: List of tuples from fetch_current_holdings_for_demat_account
    account_id: Demat account ID to filter for

Returns:
    Dict mapping security_id -> holdings info

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compute_.name: _compute_total_deltas_vectorized_per_demat
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compute_.docstring:
Compute total transaction deltas per security for a specific demat account.
Key is security_id only (since we're processing per demat account).

Returns:
    Tuple of (total_deltas dict, security_info dict)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_derive_s.name: _derive_start_holdings_vectorized_per_demat
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_derive_s.docstring:
Derive start-of-period holdings for a specific demat account using algebraic reconciliation.
Also calculates missing units (discrepancy between expected and actual).
Formula: start_units = current_units - total_delta

Args:
    starting_holdings: Holdings at the END of the period (current holdings or from oldest ledger)
    total_deltas: Net change from all transactions during the period
    security_info: Security metadata (isin_code, symbol)

Returns:
    Tuple of (start_holdings dict, missing_units dict)
    - start_holdings: Holdings at the START of the period
    - missing_units: Missing units per security (current_units - (start_units + total_delta))

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_replay_t.name: _replay_transactions_vectorized_per_demat
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_replay_t.return_type: Tuple[Dict[date, Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]]], Dict[date, Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]]]]
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_replay_t.docstring:
Replay transactions forward for a specific demat account using vectorized operations.
Groups by date and processes all transactions on each date together.

Args:
    year_txn_df: DataFrame of transactions for the year
    start_holdings: Holdings at the start of the period (key is security_id)
    account_id: Demat account ID

Returns:
    Tuple of (state_after_tx, full_state_after_tx):
    - state_after_tx: Dict mapping transaction_date -> holdings_state (only securities with transactions on that date)
    - full_state_after_tx: Dict mapping transaction_date -> full holdings_state (all securities at that point)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_entr.name: _get_entry_key
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_entr.docstring:
Generate a unique key for a ledger entry for deduplication.

Args:
    entry: Ledger entry dictionary

Returns:
    Tuple of (user_id, demat_id, security_id, ledger_date)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_int.name: _safe_int
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_int.docstring: Safely convert value to int, returning default if conversion fails or value is None/NaN.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_flo.name: _safe_float
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_flo.docstring: Safely convert value to float, returning default if conversion fails or value is None/NaN.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_str.name: _safe_str
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_str.docstring: Safely convert value to string, returning default if conversion fails or value is None/NaN.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_dat.name: _safe_date
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_safe_dat.docstring: Safely convert value to date, returning None if conversion fails.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_deduplic.docstring:
Combine and deduplicate multiple lists of ledger entries.
Entries are deduplicated based on (user_id, demat_id, security_id, ledger_date).

Args:
    *entry_lists: Variable number of entry lists to combine (processed in order)
    replace_on_conflict: If True, later entries replace earlier ones with the same key

Returns:
    Deduplicated list of entries

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_sn.name: _build_snapshot_ledger_entries
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_sn.docstring:
Build ledger entries for holdings snapshot at a specific date.
Creates ledger entries for the given date (e.g., 2025-06-17, 2025-07-01).

Args:
    holdings: Holdings at the snapshot date (key is security_id)
    account_id: Demat account ID
    snapshot_date: Date for the snapshot (e.g., date(2025, 6, 17))

Returns:
    List of ledger entry dictionaries for the snapshot date

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_st.name: _build_start_of_month_ledger_entries
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_st.docstring:
Build ledger entries for start-of-month holdings.
Creates ledger entries for the first day of the month (e.g., 2025-01-01, 2025-02-01).

Args:
    start_holdings: Holdings at the start of the month (key is security_id)
    account_id: Demat account ID
    month_start_date: First day of the month (e.g., date(2025, 1, 1))

Returns:
    List of ledger entry dictionaries for start-of-month

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_create_m.name: _create_monthly_snapshots_for_year
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_create_m.docstring:
Create monthly snapshot ledger entries for all months in a year.
Uses the most recent state before or at the start of each month.
Skips months before the first transaction date (ledger should start from first transaction).

Args:
    full_state_after_tx: Dict mapping transaction_date -> full holdings_state (all securities at that point)
    start_holdings: Holdings at the start of the year (fallback if no transactions before month start)
    account_id: Demat account ID
    year: Year to create snapshots for
    year_start: Actual start date for processing (may be mid-year)
    year_end: Actual end date for processing (may be mid-year)
    snapshot_end_date: Optional end date for snapshots (can extend beyond year_end, e.g., up to min_ledger_date - 1)

Returns:
    List of ledger entry dictionaries for monthly snapshots

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_le.name: _build_ledger_entries_vectorized_per_demat
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_build_le.docstring:
Build ledger entries from state snapshots for a specific demat account.

Args:
    state_after_tx: Dict mapping transaction_date -> holdings_state (key is security_id)

    List of ledger entry dictionaries

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_write_ye.name: _write_year_ledger_to_db
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_write_ye.docstring: Write ledger entries for a year to database with price data merge.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::generate_.name: generate_equity_ledger_from_transactions_for_account
app/service/equity.py::Equity::generate_.docstring:
Generate equity ledger entries for a specific demat account.

Supports both backward (initial onboarding) and forward (daily incremental) generation:
- Backward: Processes years newest→oldest, uses algebraic reconciliation
- Forward: Processes years oldest→newest, uses latest ledger as starting point

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID to generate ledger for
    user_id: User ID
    start_date: Start date of the period
    end_date: End date of the period
    years_to_process: Set of years to process (if None, processes all years in range)
    oldest_ledger_date: Optional ledger date to use as starting point (latest for forward, oldest for backward)

Returns:
    Dict containing processing statistics

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_ap.name: _fetch_api_transactions_for_year
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_fetch_ap.docstring:
Fetch API transactions for a specific year range.

    fi_data_id: Account fi_data_id for API calls
    year_start: Start date of the year
    year_end: End date of the year
    semaphore: Semaphore to limit parallel API calls

    user_id: User ID (optional, for Finfactor API logging)

Fetch DB transactions for a specific year range.

    year_end: End date of the year

Returns:
    List of DematAccountTransactions objects

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compare_.name: _compare_transactions
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compare_.docstring:
Compare API transactions with DB transactions using pandas for efficient comparison.

    db_transactions: List of DematAccountTransactions objects from DB
    isin_to_security_id: Mapping of ISIN to security_id

Returns:
    List of mapped transaction dicts ready for insertion (missing in DB)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compare_.name: _compare_transactions_fallback
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_compare_.docstring:
Fallback method for transaction comparison (original set-based approach).
Used if pandas comparison fails.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::reconcile.name: reconcile_account_transactions_yearwise
app/service/equity.py::Equity::reconcile.docstring:
Reconcile transactions for an account yearwise (2025 → 2024 → ... → 2005).
Compares API transactions with DB transactions for each year.
If discrepancies found, inserts missing transactions and regenerates ledger.

Args:
    db: Database session
    account_id: Demat account ID
    user_id: User ID
    phone_number: User's phone number (for API calls)
    fi_data_id: Account fi_data_id (for API calls)

Returns:
    Dict with reconciliation results:
    - discrepancies_found: bool
    - missing_in_db_count: int
    - discrepancy_years: List[int]
    - ledger_deleted: bool
    - deleted_count: int
    - ledger_regenerated: bool
    - rows_generated: int
    - error: Optional[str]

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.name: _process_reconciliation_years
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_process_.docstring:
Process reconciliation for all years and collect discrepancies.

Returns:
    Dict with:
    - all_missing_transactions: List of all missing transactions
    - discrepancy_years: List of years with discrepancies
    - max_discrepancy_date: Latest date where discrepancy was found

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_reconcil.name: _reconcile_single_year
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_reconcil.docstring:
Reconcile transactions for a single year.

Returns:
    Dict with:
    - missing_transactions: List of missing transactions (empty if none)

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_isin.name: _get_isin_to_security_id_mapping
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_get_isin.docstring: Extract ISINs from API statements and get security_id mapping.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_insert_m.name: _insert_missing_transactions
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_insert_m.docstring: Insert missing transactions for the year.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_update_m.name: _update_max_discrepancy_date

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_regenera.name: _regenerate_ledger_for_discrepancies
app/service/equity.py::Equity::_regenera.docstring: Regenerate ledger when discrepancies are found.

app/service/equity.py::Equity::_calculat.name: _calculate_deletion_end_date

app/service/equity_price_history.py.name: equity_price_history.py
app/service/equity_price_history.py.docstring:
Equity Price History Service

Service layer for fetching and managing equity price history data from Morningstar API.
Handles both historical data backfill and daily updates with optimized parallel processing.

Performance optimizations:
- Historical sync: 50 API calls/second with 10 parallel DB connections
- Daily sync: 50 symbols per API call
- Bulk initialization: Parallel processing with optimized batching (1500 records per batch)

app/service/equity_price_history.py::Sim.name: SimpleRateLimiter
app/service/equity_price_history.py::Sim.docstring:
Allow `rate` acquisitions per `per` seconds.
All API calls go through acquire() so QPS is enforced centrally.

app/service/equity_price_history.py::Equ.name: EquityPriceHistoryService
app/service/equity_price_history.py::Equ.docstring: Service for equity price history operations with optimized parallel processing.

app/service/equity_price_history.py::Equ.name: _split_into_batches
